The most common fix is short-circuited iat sensor, but a proper diagnostic is needed to confirm exactly what needs doing. Labour rates vary across the country, with London and the South East generally being more expensive than other regions.

motFail: The engine warning light alone does not cause an automatic MOT failure in the UK. However, if this fault affects exhaust emissions or causes visible smoke, it could result in a fail on the emissions test.

name.fr: Capteur de temperature du liquide de refroidissement — tension basse

description.fr: Le capteur de temperature du liquide de refroidissement (ECT) est essentiel pour le bon fonctionnement du moteur. Le P0117 signale une tension basse sur ce capteur, ce qui est interprete comme une temperature extremement elevee. Le calculateur reagit en enrichissant le melange et en activant les ventilateurs a pleine puissance. Si c'est un faux signal du capteur (le moteur n'est pas vraiment en surchauffe), vous aurez les ventilateurs qui tournent en permanence, une surconsommation et un fonctionnement altere. Verifiez la temperature reelle avec un thermometre infrarouge sur le bloc moteur et comparez avec la valeur affichee. Si le moteur est effectivement froid mais que le capteur indique surchauffe, c'est le capteur qui est en court-circuit. Sinon, vous avez un vrai probleme de refroidissement a traiter en urgence.
description.en: A P0117 fault code has been detected by your car's engine management system, indicating a problem with coolant temperature sensor — low voltage. The ECU is essentially the brain of your engine, and when it spots something out of the ordinary, it stores this code to help your mechanic diagnose the issue. You may have noticed the engine warning light on your dashboard, and perhaps some changes in how the car feels — maybe a slight judder at idle, a moment of hesitation when you press the accelerator, or a general feeling that the engine is not quite as responsive as usual. These are all typical signs of an engine management issue. The good news is that most of these problems have well-understood causes and straightforward fixes. It could be anything from a dirty sensor to a worn spark plug or a small air leak in the intake system. A proper OBD2 diagnostic at your local garage will identify exactly what is going on, and in many cases the repair is quicker and cheaper than you might fear. Do get it looked at sooner rather than later to avoid any knock-on problems.

name.en: Wiring short circuite at the ground

fr.question: Les ventilateurs tournent tout le temps, c'est normal ?
fr.answer: Non, c'est un symptome typique du P0117. Le calculateur pense que le moteur surchauffe et active les ventilateurs en permanence. Si le moteur n'est pas reellement chaud, c'est le capteur qui est en cause.

fr.question: Comment differencier un faux signal d'une vraie surchauffe ?
fr.answer: Touchez prudemment le haut du radiateur. S'il est froid ou tiede alors que le capteur dit 110 degres, c'est un faux signal. Utilisez un thermometre infrarouge pour etre precis.

fr.question: Le capteur ECT coute-t-il cher ?
fr.answer: Non, entre 10 et 40 euros pour la piece. C'est un petit capteur visse dans le bloc moteur ou le boitier de thermostat. La main d'oeuvre est de 15 a 30 minutes en general.

en.question: What does the P0117 fault code mean?
en.answer: The P0117 code means your car's computer has detected a problem with coolant temperature sensor — low voltage. This is a medium-severity engine fault. Your mechanic can use an OBD2 diagnostic scanner to investigate further and identify the specific cause.

en.question: Can I drive with a P0117 code?
en.answer: Yes, you can generally continue driving with this code. It is a medium-severity fault that should not cause immediate danger. However, you may notice some changes in performance or fuel consumption. Book a diagnostic appointment when convenient — the sooner you get it sorted, the less likely it is to cause further issues.

en.question: Will P0117 fail my MOT?
en.answer: The engine warning light alone does not cause an automatic MOT failure in the UK. However, if this fault affects exhaust emissions or causes visible smoke, it could result in a fail on the emissions test.

en.question: How much does P0117 repair cost in the UK?
en.answer: In the UK, repair costs for a P0117 fault typically range from around £17 to £674, depending on the root cause and your vehicle's make and model. The most common fix is coolant temperature sensor short circuit, but a proper diagnostic is needed to confirm exactly what needs doing. Labour rates vary across the country, with London and the South East generally being more expensive than other regions.

motFail: The engine warning light alone does not cause an automatic MOT failure in the UK. However, if this fault affects exhaust emissions or causes visible smoke, it could result in a fail on the emissions test.

name.fr: Capteur de temperature du liquide de refroidissement — tension haute

description.fr: Le P0118 indique une tension elevee sur le capteur de temperature du liquide de refroidissement, ce que le calculateur interprete comme une temperature extremement basse. Le moteur se comporte alors comme s'il etait toujours a froid : le melange est enrichi en permanence, le ralenti est plus haut que la normale et le chauffage ne fonctionne pas correctement malgre un moteur chaud. C'est generalement un fil coupe ou un connecteur debranche qui cause ce probleme, car un circuit ouvert donne une tension maximale sur ce type de capteur. Si le capteur vient d'etre remplace, verifiez que le bon modele a ete installe car une resistance nominale differente peut aussi provoquer ce code. Le diagnostic est simple avec un multimetre : mesurez la resistance du capteur et comparez-la aux specifications du constructeur.
description.en: The diagnostic trouble code P0118 relates to coolant temperature sensor — high voltage, which is a fault within the engine management system. Your car uses dozens of sensors and actuators to ensure the engine runs efficiently, and when one of these components starts to play up, the ECU stores a fault code and turns on the engine warning light. This is your car's way of asking for a bit of attention. Common symptoms you might experience include rough idling, slight misfires, reduced acceleration, or increased fuel consumption. While many engine fault codes do not require you to stop driving immediately, it is always wise to get the issue investigated promptly. A trained technician will connect a diagnostic tool to your car's OBD2 port, read the code, and examine the live data to determine the exact cause. Repairs vary from simple maintenance tasks like replacing spark plugs or cleaning sensors, to more involved work on components like the throttle body or fuel injectors. Whatever the cause, addressing it early will save you money in the long run and keep your car running at its best.

fr.question: Les ventilateurs ne s'activent jamais, c'est lie ?
fr.answer: Oui, si le calculateur pense que le moteur est toujours froid, il n'active jamais les ventilateurs. C'est un risque de surchauffe reelle si vous restez dans les embouteillages.

fr.question: Ma consommation a augmente, est-ce le capteur ECT ?
fr.answer: C'est possible. Un capteur qui indique toujours froid pousse le calculateur a enrichir le melange en permanence, comme lors d'un demarrage a froid. Cela peut augmenter la consommation de 15 a 30%.

fr.question: Comment verifier le capteur ECT avec un multimetre ?
fr.answer: Debranchez le connecteur et mesurez la resistance aux bornes du capteur. A 20 degres environ, attendez-vous a 2000-3000 ohms. A 80 degres, la resistance devrait etre autour de 300-400 ohms. Circuit ouvert = capteur HS.

en.question: What does the P0118 fault code mean?
en.answer: The P0118 code means your car's computer has detected a problem with coolant temperature sensor — high voltage. This is a low-severity engine fault. Your mechanic can use an OBD2 diagnostic scanner to investigate further and identify the specific cause.

en.question: Can I drive with a P0118 code?
en.answer: Yes, you can generally continue driving with this code. It is a low-severity fault that should not cause immediate danger. However, you may notice some changes in performance or fuel consumption. Book a diagnostic appointment when convenient — the sooner you get it sorted, the less likely it is to cause further issues.

en.question: Will P0118 fail my MOT?
en.answer: The engine warning light alone does not cause an automatic MOT failure in the UK. However, if this fault affects exhaust emissions or causes visible smoke, it could result in a fail on the emissions test.

en.question: How much does P0118 repair cost in the UK?
en.answer: In the UK, repair costs for a P0118 fault typically range from around £13 to £69, depending on the root cause and your vehicle's make and model. The most common fix is coolant temperature sensor open circuit, but a proper diagnostic is needed to confirm exactly what needs doing. Labour rates vary across the country, with London and the South East generally being more expensive than other regions.

motFail: The engine warning light alone does not cause an automatic MOT failure in the UK. However, if this fault affects exhaust emissions or causes visible smoke, it could result in a fail on the emissions test.

name.fr: Capteur de position du papillon A — plage/performance

description.fr: Le P0121 signale que le signal du capteur de position du papillon principal (capteur A) est hors de sa plage attendue ou que ses performances sont degradees. Le calculateur compare la position du papillon avec la position de la pedale d'accelerateur et le regime moteur. Si les valeurs ne correspondent pas, ce code est enregistre. Un papillon encrasse est la cause la plus frequente : les depots sur l'axe du papillon genent son mouvement et le capteur renvoie des valeurs incoherentes. Un nettoyage du boitier papillon avec un produit adapte resout souvent le probleme. Sinon, la piste resistive du capteur peut etre usee, surtout dans la zone de ralenti ou le papillon passe le plus de temps. Un mode degrade peut s'activer, limitant fortement les performances du moteur.
description.en: When your car's computer stores a P0121 code, it is telling you there is a problem with throttle position sensor a — range/performance. This is an engine-related fault, and while it might sound alarming, many engine codes are more of a nuisance than an emergency. Your engine management system is incredibly sophisticated — it monitors hundreds of parameters every second to keep everything running smoothly. When something drifts outside the normal range, the system logs a fault code and illuminates the engine warning light on your dashboard. Depending on the severity, you might notice symptoms like rough running, hesitation during acceleration, slightly higher fuel consumption, or an unstable idle. In many cases, you can continue to drive carefully, but it is important to get the car checked as soon as reasonably possible. Ignoring engine faults can lead to secondary damage — for example, a misfire left unchecked can damage the catalytic converter over time. A good mechanic with proper diagnostic equipment will be able to read the code and the associated live data to identify the root cause and get you back on the road with confidence.

fr.question: Nettoyer le papillon suffit-il a resoudre le P0121 ?
fr.answer: Dans environ 50% des cas oui. Utilisez un nettoyant pour corps de papillon, nettoyez la zone du volet et laissez secher completement. Apres remontage, un reapprentissage du ralenti est souvent necessaire.

fr.question: Qu'est-ce qu'une piste resistive usee ?
fr.answer: Le capteur TPS utilise un curseur qui glisse sur une piste en carbone ou en ceramique conductrice. A force, la piste s'use a l'endroit le plus sollicite, creant une zone morte ou le signal saute.

fr.question: Le mode degrade s'active-t-il a chaque fois ?
fr.answer: Pas necessairement. Le mode degrade s'active si le calculateur juge que la difference entre les capteurs A et B est trop grande pour etre fiable. Un encrassement leger peut juste causer des a-coups.

en.question: What does the P0121 fault code mean?
en.answer: The P0121 code means your car's computer has detected a problem with throttle position sensor a — range/performance. This is a medium-severity engine fault. Your mechanic can use an OBD2 diagnostic scanner to investigate further and identify the specific cause.

en.question: Can I drive with a P0121 code?
en.answer: Yes, you can generally continue driving with this code. It is a medium-severity fault that should not cause immediate danger. However, you may notice some changes in performance or fuel consumption. Book a diagnostic appointment when convenient — the sooner you get it sorted, the less likely it is to cause further issues.

en.question: Will P0121 fail my MOT?
en.answer: The engine warning light alone does not cause an automatic MOT failure in the UK. However, if this fault affects exhaust emissions or causes visible smoke, it could result in a fail on the emissions test.

en.question: How much does P0121 repair cost in the UK?
en.answer: In the UK, repair costs for a P0121 fault typically range from around £26 to £479, depending on the root cause and your vehicle's make and model. The most common fix is dirty throttle body, but a proper diagnostic is needed to confirm exactly what needs doing. Labour rates vary across the country, with London and the South East generally being more expensive than other regions.

motFail: The engine warning light alone does not cause an automatic MOT failure in the UK. However, if this fault affects exhaust emissions or causes visible smoke, it could result in a fail on the emissions test.

name.fr: Capteur de position du papillon A — tension basse
name.en: Throttle position sensor A — low voltage

description.fr: Le P0122 indique que le capteur principal du papillon des gaz envoie une tension trop basse au calculateur. La tension de sortie d'un capteur TPS varie normalement entre 0.5V au repos et 4.5V a pleine ouverture. Une tension inferieure a 0.5V signale un probleme electrique : court-circuit a la masse dans le cablage, capteur endommage, ou connecteur corrode. Le calculateur ne peut pas determiner si le conducteur appuie sur l'accelerateur ou non, ce qui est evidemment dangereux. Le mode degrade s'active immediatement et la puissance est drastiquement limitee. Avant de remplacer quoi que ce soit, verifiez l'etat du connecteur du boitier papillon. Un simple nettoyage des broches avec un nettoyant contacts peut parfois resoudre le probleme si la corrosion etait en cause.
description.en: A P0122 fault code has been detected by your car's engine management system, indicating a problem with throttle position sensor a — low voltage. The ECU is essentially the brain of your engine, and when it spots something out of the ordinary, it stores this code to help your mechanic diagnose the issue. You may have noticed the engine warning light on your dashboard, and perhaps some changes in how the car feels — maybe a slight judder at idle, a moment of hesitation when you press the accelerator, or a general feeling that the engine is not quite as responsive as usual. These are all typical signs of an engine management issue. The good news is that most of these problems have well-understood causes and straightforward fixes. It could be anything from a dirty sensor to a worn spark plug or a small air leak in the intake system. A proper OBD2 diagnostic at your local garage will identify exactly what is going on, and in many cases the repair is quicker and cheaper than you might fear. Do get it looked at sooner rather than later to avoid any knock-on problems.

name.fr: Cablage en court-circuit a la masse
name.en: Wiring in short circuit at the ground

fr.question: Ma voiture n'accelere presque plus, est-ce le P0122 ?
fr.answer: Tres probablement. Le mode degrade limite la puissance a un minimum pour vous permettre de vous rendre au garage. La vitesse maximale est souvent de 40-50 km/h.

fr.question: Peut-on remettre le mode normal en effacant le code ?
fr.answer: Temporairement peut-etre, mais si le capteur est vraiment defaillant, le code reviendra rapidement. Mieux vaut reparer la cause plutot que d'effacer les symptomes.

fr.question: Le capteur TPS est-il le meme que le capteur pedale ?
fr.answer: Non, ce sont deux capteurs differents. Le capteur TPS mesure la position du volet papillon lui-meme. Le capteur pedale mesure la demande du conducteur. Les deux doivent correspondre pour un fonctionnement normal.

en.question: What does the P0122 fault code mean?
en.answer: The P0122 code means your car's computer has detected a problem with throttle position sensor a — low voltage. This is a high-severity engine fault. Your mechanic can use an OBD2 diagnostic scanner to investigate further and identify the specific cause.

en.question: Can I drive with a P0122 code?
en.answer: You can drive short distances with this code, but it is advisable to get it checked soon. This is a high-severity fault that could worsen over time. Avoid long motorway journeys until a mechanic has had a look, and keep an eye on your temperature gauge and warning lights.

en.question: Will P0122 fail my MOT?
en.answer: This code may cause an MOT failure if it results in excessive emissions or visible smoke. The engine warning light being illuminated is not an automatic MOT fail in the UK, but the underlying fault could affect emissions readings.

en.question: How much does P0122 repair cost in the UK?
en.answer: In the UK, repair costs for a P0122 fault typically range from around £13 to £165, depending on the root cause and your vehicle's make and model. The most common fix is internally short-circuited throttle position sensor, but a proper diagnostic is needed to confirm exactly what needs doing. Labour rates vary across the country, with London and the South East generally being more expensive than other regions.

motFail: This code may cause an MOT failure if it results in excessive emissions or visible smoke. The engine warning light being illuminated is not an automatic MOT fail in the UK, but the underlying fault could affect emissions readings.

description.fr: Le P0130 est un code general qui indique un probleme sur le circuit de la sonde lambda amont de la banque 1. Cette sonde est la plus importante pour le reglage du melange air-carburant en temps reel. Le calculateur envoie une tension de reference a la sonde et attend une reponse qui oscille entre 0.1V (melange pauvre) et 0.9V (melange riche). Si le signal est absent, fixe ou hors plage, le P0130 est enregistre. Le moteur fonctionne alors en boucle ouverte, c'est-a-dire avec des valeurs de carburant pre-programmees qui ne sont pas optimales. La consommation augmente, les emissions aussi, et le catalyseur souffre a la longue. Le remplacement de la sonde est la solution la plus courante, mais verifiez d'abord le cablage qui peut s'user a cause de la chaleur de l'echappement.
description.en: The P0130 diagnostic trouble code indicates that your vehicle's emissions control system has flagged up an issue with upstream lambda sensor — circuit fault (bank 1). Your car's ECU constantly monitors exhaust gas composition using sensors before and after the catalytic converter. When the readings suggest the system is not cleaning up exhaust gases effectively, this fault code is stored. From a driving perspective, you may notice a slight drop in fuel economy or perhaps a rotten egg smell from the exhaust on occasion. The key concern here is the MOT test — emissions failures are one of the most common reasons cars fail, and this code directly relates to that. A trained technician can run a proper diagnostic to identify whether it is a sensor issue, a catalytic converter problem, or something simpler like an exhaust leak. Most of these repairs are straightforward and well within the capabilities of any decent garage. Getting it fixed promptly will keep your running costs down and ensure a smooth MOT experience.

name.fr: Sonde lambda amont en fin de vie
Cablage brule par la chaleur de l'echappement
name.en: Wiring brule by the chaleur of the exhaust

name.fr: Connecteur de sonde debranche ou oxyde
name.en: Connector of sensor disconnected or oxidised

fr.question: Qu'est-ce que la boucle ouverte et la boucle fermee ?
fr.answer: En boucle fermee, le calculateur ajuste le melange en temps reel grace a la sonde lambda. En boucle ouverte, il utilise des valeurs fixes pre-programmees. La boucle ouverte est moins precise et augmente consommation et emissions.

fr.question: Combien de temps dure une sonde lambda ?
fr.answer: En moyenne 100 000 a 150 000 km pour une sonde classique. Les sondes a large bande des vehicules recents peuvent durer plus longtemps, mais la chaleur et les cycles thermiques les usent inevitablement.

fr.question: Peut-on installer une sonde lambda universelle ?
fr.answer: Oui, pour les sondes classiques a zircone. Il faut souder le connecteur de l'ancienne sonde sur la nouvelle. Pour les sondes a large bande, il est preferable d'utiliser la reference exacte du constructeur.

en.question: What does the P0130 fault code mean?
en.answer: The P0130 code means your car's computer has detected a problem with upstream lambda sensor — circuit fault (bank 1). This is a medium-severity emissions fault. Your mechanic can use an OBD2 diagnostic scanner to investigate further and identify the specific cause.

en.question: Can I drive with a P0130 code?
en.answer: Yes, you can generally continue driving with this code. It is a medium-severity fault that should not cause immediate danger. However, you may notice some changes in performance or fuel consumption. Book a diagnostic appointment when convenient — the sooner you get it sorted, the less likely it is to cause further issues.

en.question: Will P0130 fail my MOT?
en.answer: Yes, this code is very likely to cause an MOT failure. Emissions are tested during the MOT, and a fault in the emissions system typically results in elevated exhaust gas readings that exceed the legal limits.

en.question: How much does P0130 repair cost in the UK?
en.answer: In the UK, repair costs for a P0130 fault typically range from around £8 to £261, depending on the root cause and your vehicle's make and model. The most common fix is end-of-life upstream lambda sensor, but a proper diagnostic is needed to confirm exactly what needs doing. Labour rates vary across the country, with London and the South East generally being more expensive than other regions.

motFail: Yes, this code is very likely to cause an MOT failure. Emissions are tested during the MOT, and a fault in the emissions system typically results in elevated exhaust gas readings that exceed the legal limits.

description.fr: Le P0133 indique que la sonde lambda amont met trop de temps a reagir aux changements du melange air-carburant. Une sonde en bon etat oscille rapidement entre riche et pauvre — typiquement 5 a 10 fois par seconde. Quand elle vieillit, ses reactions ralentissent et le calculateur detecte que les transitions sont trop lentes. Le probleme, c'est que le calculateur essaie d'ajuster le melange en temps reel, et si la sonde repond avec un retard, les corrections arrivent trop tard. C'est comme conduire en regardant dans le retro au lieu du pare-brise. Le resultat est un melange qui oscille entre trop riche et trop pauvre, ce qui augmente la consommation et les emissions. Le remplacement de la sonde est la solution la plus fiable. Un nettoyage peut parfois prolonger sa vie, mais c'est temporaire.
description.en: When your car shows a P0133 fault code, it means something in the emissions system is not quite right. Specifically, the issue relates to upstream lambda sensor — slow response (bank 1). Your car's onboard computer, known as the ECU, monitors how cleanly exhaust gases leave the engine. When the readings fall outside the expected range, this code gets stored and the engine warning light comes on. This is an important issue because emissions are tested during your MOT, and a fault here could result in a fail. In most cases, you can still drive the car safely, but you may notice slightly higher fuel consumption or an unusual smell from the exhaust. Common culprits include a worn catalytic converter, ageing lambda sensors, or exhaust leaks. The good news is that a qualified mechanic with an OBD2 scanner can usually pinpoint the root cause fairly quickly, and many repairs are more affordable than you might expect. Do not ignore this code — getting it sorted sooner rather than later will save you money and help your car pass its next MOT without any drama.

name.fr: Sonde empoisonnee par du silicone ou du plomb
name.en: Sensor contaminated by silicone or lead

fr.question: Comment sait-on que la sonde est lente ?
fr.answer: Avec un outil OBD2, on observe les oscillations de la sonde en temps reel. Une sonde rapide oscille 5 a 10 fois par seconde. Une sonde lente oscille 1 a 2 fois par seconde ou reste figee.

fr.question: Le silicone peut-il endommager la sonde ?
fr.answer: Oui, le silicone (present dans certains joints et sprays) contamine la surface reactive de la sonde et la rend paresseuse. Evitez d'utiliser du silicone pres du circuit d'admission ou d'echappement.

fr.question: Un nettoyage peut-il sauver une sonde paresseuse ?
fr.answer: Parfois, un nettoyage prolonge au decapant four (trempage 8h) peut redonner un peu de reactivite. Mais c'est temporaire — si la sonde est lente, elle est en fin de vie et le remplacement est la solution durable.

en.question: What does the P0133 fault code mean?
en.answer: The P0133 code means your car's computer has detected a problem with upstream lambda sensor — slow response (bank 1). This is a low-severity emissions fault. Your mechanic can use an OBD2 diagnostic scanner to investigate further and identify the specific cause.

en.question: Can I drive with a P0133 code?
en.answer: Yes, you can generally continue driving with this code. It is a low-severity fault that should not cause immediate danger. However, you may notice some changes in performance or fuel consumption. Book a diagnostic appointment when convenient — the sooner you get it sorted, the less likely it is to cause further issues.

en.question: Will P0133 fail my MOT?
en.answer: Yes, this code is very likely to cause an MOT failure. Emissions are tested during the MOT, and a fault in the emissions system typically results in elevated exhaust gas readings that exceed the legal limits.

en.question: How much does P0133 repair cost in the UK?
en.answer: In the UK, repair costs for a P0133 fault typically range from around £68 to £251, depending on the root cause and your vehicle's make and model. The most common fix is ageing lambda sensor, but a proper diagnostic is needed to confirm exactly what needs doing. Labour rates vary across the country, with London and the South East generally being more expensive than other regions.

motFail: Yes, this code is very likely to cause an MOT failure. Emissions are tested during the MOT, and a fault in the emissions system typically results in elevated exhaust gas readings that exceed the legal limits.

name.fr: Sonde lambda amont — aucune activite detectee (banque 1)
name.en: Upstream lambda sensor — no activity detected (bank 1)

description.fr: Le P0134 signifie que la sonde lambda amont ne montre aucune activite — son signal reste fixe et ne varie pas du tout. C'est plus grave qu'une sonde lente (P0133) car ici la sonde ne reagit absolument plus aux changements de melange. Le calculateur ne peut plus du tout utiliser son retour pour ajuster le dosage air-carburant. Le moteur fonctionne en boucle ouverte permanente, ce qui degradera la consommation et les emissions de maniere significative. Les causes sont souvent les memes qu'un P0130 : sonde en fin de vie, chauffage defaillant qui empeche la sonde d'atteindre sa temperature de fonctionnement, ou probleme electrique sur le circuit. Si la sonde est neuve et que le code persiste, verifiez que le bon modele a ete installe et que le chauffage est correctement alimente.
description.en: A P0134 code appearing on your dashboard means the engine management system has detected a problem with upstream lambda sensor — no activity detected (bank 1). This is part of the emissions control system, which ensures your car meets environmental standards. When this system is not working properly, your vehicle may produce more harmful gases than allowed, and this is something the MOT test will pick up during the emissions check. You might not notice much difference in how the car drives day to day — perhaps a slight increase in fuel consumption or a faint smell from the tailpipe. However, it is well worth getting this looked at promptly. A good garage will use diagnostic equipment to read the live data from your engine and determine exactly what needs attention. Repairs can range from a simple sensor replacement to something more involved like a catalytic converter swap, but your mechanic will be able to give you an honest assessment. The sooner you act, the less likely the problem is to snowball into something more expensive down the line.

fr.question: Aucune activite, ca veut dire que la sonde est morte ?
fr.answer: Pas forcement. La sonde peut etre OK mais ne pas chauffer assez (element chauffant HS) ou avoir un probleme de cablage. Verifiez le chauffage et le cablage avant de remplacer la sonde.

fr.question: Combien consomme-t-on en plus avec un P0134 ?
fr.answer: En boucle ouverte, la surconsommation peut atteindre 20 a 30% car le calculateur utilise des valeurs par defaut generalement trop riches pour proteger le moteur et le catalyseur.

fr.question: Ce code empeche-t-il de passer le controle technique ?
fr.answer: Oui, dans tous les pays qui controlent les emissions. Un voyant moteur allume est un motif de refus, et les emissions seront effectivement degradees par le fonctionnement en boucle ouverte.

en.question: What does the P0134 fault code mean?
en.answer: The P0134 code means your car's computer has detected a problem with upstream lambda sensor — no activity detected (bank 1). This is a medium-severity emissions fault. Your mechanic can use an OBD2 diagnostic scanner to investigate further and identify the specific cause.

en.question: Can I drive with a P0134 code?
en.answer: Yes, you can generally continue driving with this code. It is a medium-severity fault that should not cause immediate danger. However, you may notice some changes in performance or fuel consumption. Book a diagnostic appointment when convenient — the sooner you get it sorted, the less likely it is to cause further issues.

en.question: Will P0134 fail my MOT?
en.answer: Yes, this code is very likely to cause an MOT failure. Emissions are tested during the MOT, and a fault in the emissions system typically results in elevated exhaust gas readings that exceed the legal limits.

en.question: How much does P0134 repair cost in the UK?
en.answer: In the UK, repair costs for a P0134 fault typically range from around £25 to £253, depending on the root cause and your vehicle's make and model. The most common fix is completely failed lambda sensor, but a proper diagnostic is needed to confirm exactly what needs doing. Labour rates vary across the country, with London and the South East generally being more expensive than other regions.

motFail: Yes, this code is very likely to cause an MOT failure. Emissions are tested during the MOT, and a fault in the emissions system typically results in elevated exhaust gas readings that exceed the legal limits.

description.fr: Le P0137 signale que la sonde lambda situee apres le catalyseur envoie une tension anormalement basse. Apres le catalyseur, la sonde devrait montrer un signal relativement stable autour de 0.5-0.7V si le catalyseur fonctionne bien. Une tension constamment basse peut indiquer que le catalyseur laisse passer un melange pauvre, ou que la sonde elle-meme est defaillante. C'est un code qui peut apparaitre seul ou en compagnie du fameux P0420 si le catalyseur est effectivement use. Le calculateur utilise cette sonde uniquement pour surveiller le catalyseur, donc le fonctionnement du moteur n'est pas directement affecte. La conduite reste normale mais les emissions et le controle technique seront impactes. Avant de remplacer la sonde, verifiez qu'il n'y a pas de fuite d'echappement entre le catalyseur et la sonde qui fausserait la mesure.
description.en: The P0137 diagnostic trouble code indicates that your vehicle's emissions control system has flagged up an issue with downstream lambda sensor — low voltage (bank 1). Your car's ECU constantly monitors exhaust gas composition using sensors before and after the catalytic converter. When the readings suggest the system is not cleaning up exhaust gases effectively, this fault code is stored. From a driving perspective, you may notice a slight drop in fuel economy or perhaps a rotten egg smell from the exhaust on occasion. The key concern here is the MOT test — emissions failures are one of the most common reasons cars fail, and this code directly relates to that. A trained technician can run a proper diagnostic to identify whether it is a sensor issue, a catalytic converter problem, or something simpler like an exhaust leak. Most of these repairs are straightforward and well within the capabilities of any decent garage. Getting it fixed promptly will keep your running costs down and ensure a smooth MOT experience.

name.fr: Fuite d'echappement pres de la sonde

fr.question: Le P0137 signifie-t-il que le catalyseur est mort ?
fr.answer: Pas necessairement. Si la sonde est defaillante, elle donne une lecture fausse. Si le P0420 est aussi present, la probabilite d'un catalyseur use augmente significativement.

fr.question: Comment tester la sonde aval ?
fr.answer: Avec un outil OBD2, observez la tension de la sonde aval en temps reel. Elle doit etre relativement stable entre 0.5V et 0.8V. Si elle reste figee pres de 0V, la sonde est probablement HS.

fr.question: Faut-il remplacer la sonde ou le catalyseur ?
fr.answer: Commencez toujours par la sonde car c'est beaucoup moins cher. Si le code revient apres remplacement de la sonde et que le P0420 est present, le catalyseur est probablement le coupable.

en.question: What does the P0137 fault code mean?
en.answer: The P0137 code means your car's computer has detected a problem with downstream lambda sensor — low voltage (bank 1). This is a low-severity emissions fault. Your mechanic can use an OBD2 diagnostic scanner to investigate further and identify the specific cause.

en.question: Can I drive with a P0137 code?
en.answer: Yes, you can generally continue driving with this code. It is a low-severity fault that should not cause immediate danger. However, you may notice some changes in performance or fuel consumption. Book a diagnostic appointment when convenient — the sooner you get it sorted, the less likely it is to cause further issues.

en.question: Will P0137 fail my MOT?

en.question: How much does P0137 repair cost in the UK?
en.answer: In the UK, repair costs for a P0137 fault typically range from around £44 to £1316, depending on the root cause and your vehicle's make and model. The most common fix is faulty downstream lambda sensor, but a proper diagnostic is needed to confirm exactly what needs doing. Labour rates vary across the country, with London and the South East generally being more expensive than other regions.

motFail: Yes, this code is very likely to cause an MOT failure. Emissions are tested during the MOT, and a fault in the emissions system typically results in elevated exhaust gas readings that exceed the legal limits.

description.fr: Le P0138 signale une tension trop haute sur la sonde lambda aval. Cela indique soit une sonde qui est bloquee en position riche, soit un melange reellement trop riche qui arrive apres le catalyseur. Si le catalyseur ne convertit plus correctement les gaz, le melange riche passe au travers et la sonde aval le detecte. Mais la sonde elle-meme peut aussi etre en court-circuit interne ou contamee, donnant un faux signal haut. Un bon diagnostic commence par verifier les corrections carburant et l'etat de la sonde amont. Si la sonde amont et les trims sont normaux, c'est probablement la sonde aval qui est en cause. Si les trims sont fortement negatifs (le calculateur essaie de reduire le carburant), il y a un vrai probleme de melange riche a investiguer en amont.
description.en: When your car shows a P0138 fault code, it means something in the emissions system is not quite right. Specifically, the issue relates to downstream lambda sensor — high voltage (bank 1). Your car's onboard computer, known as the ECU, monitors how cleanly exhaust gases leave the engine. When the readings fall outside the expected range, this code gets stored and the engine warning light comes on. This is an important issue because emissions are tested during your MOT, and a fault here could result in a fail. In most cases, you can still drive the car safely, but you may notice slightly higher fuel consumption or an unusual smell from the exhaust. Common culprits include a worn catalytic converter, ageing lambda sensors, or exhaust leaks. The good news is that a qualified mechanic with an OBD2 scanner can usually pinpoint the root cause fairly quickly, and many repairs are more affordable than you might expect. Do not ignore this code — getting it sorted sooner rather than later will save you money and help your car pass its next MOT without any drama.

fr.question: Tension haute sur la sonde aval, c'est grave ?
fr.answer: Pas pour la conduite au quotidien, mais ca indique un dysfonctionnement du systeme de depollution. Le voyant restera allume et le controle technique sera rate.

fr.question: Comment savoir si c'est la sonde ou un vrai melange riche ?
fr.answer: Regardez les corrections carburant (fuel trims). Si elles sont normales, c'est la sonde aval qui ment. Si elles sont tres negatives, il y a un vrai exces de carburant a investiguer.

fr.question: Un catalyseur sature, ca se repare ?
fr.answer: Un catalyseur n'est pas reparable. Quand il est sature, il faut le remplacer. Certains traitements de decalaminage peuvent prolonger un peu sa vie s'il n'est pas completement mort.

en.question: What does the P0138 fault code mean?
en.answer: The P0138 code means your car's computer has detected a problem with downstream lambda sensor — high voltage (bank 1). This is a low-severity emissions fault. Your mechanic can use an OBD2 diagnostic scanner to investigate further and identify the specific cause.

en.question: Can I drive with a P0138 code?
en.answer: Yes, you can generally continue driving with this code. It is a low-severity fault that should not cause immediate danger. However, you may notice some changes in performance or fuel consumption. Book a diagnostic appointment when convenient — the sooner you get it sorted, the less likely it is to cause further issues.

en.question: Will P0138 fail my MOT?
en.answer: Yes, this code is very likely to cause an MOT failure. Emissions are tested during the MOT, and a fault in the emissions system typically results in elevated exhaust gas readings that exceed the legal limits.

en.question: How much does P0138 repair cost in the UK?
en.answer: In the UK, repair costs for a P0138 fault typically range from around £69 to £1246, depending on the root cause and your vehicle's make and model. The most common fix is short-circuited downstream lambda sensor, but a proper diagnostic is needed to confirm exactly what needs doing. Labour rates vary across the country, with London and the South East generally being more expensive than other regions.

motFail: Yes, this code is very likely to cause an MOT failure. Emissions are tested during the MOT, and a fault in the emissions system typically results in elevated exhaust gas readings that exceed the legal limits.

description.fr: Le P0139 indique que la sonde lambda aval reagit trop lentement aux changements du melange sortant du catalyseur. Normalement, la sonde aval oscille beaucoup moins que la sonde amont — c'est meme signe d'un bon catalyseur. Mais si elle ne reagit plus du tout ou tres lentement a des variations volontaires du melange lors des tests du calculateur, ce code est enregistre. La sonde vieillit comme toute piece mecanique et son temps de reponse augmente avec les kilometres. Ce code est souvent le signe qu'il est temps de remplacer la sonde aval. Il n'a aucun impact sur la conduite car la sonde aval ne participe pas au reglage du melange. Neanmoins il faut s'en occuper pour le controle technique et pour que le calculateur puisse continuer a surveiller l'etat du catalyseur.
description.en: A P0139 code appearing on your dashboard means the engine management system has detected a problem with downstream lambda sensor — slow response (bank 1). This is part of the emissions control system, which ensures your car meets environmental standards. When this system is not working properly, your vehicle may produce more harmful gases than allowed, and this is something the MOT test will pick up during the emissions check. You might not notice much difference in how the car drives day to day — perhaps a slight increase in fuel consumption or a faint smell from the tailpipe. However, it is well worth getting this looked at promptly. A good garage will use diagnostic equipment to read the live data from your engine and determine exactly what needs attention. Repairs can range from a simple sensor replacement to something more involved like a catalytic converter swap, but your mechanic will be able to give you an honest assessment. The sooner you act, the less likely the problem is to snowball into something more expensive down the line.

fr.question: La sonde aval est-elle censee osciller ?
fr.answer: Beaucoup moins que la sonde amont. Un bon catalyseur lisse les oscillations, donc la sonde aval doit etre relativement stable. Mais elle doit quand meme reagir aux changements lors des tests du calculateur.

fr.question: Ce code affecte-t-il les performances ?
fr.answer: Non, la sonde aval ne participe pas au reglage du melange. Votre voiture roulera exactement de la meme maniere. C'est un code purement lie a la surveillance du catalyseur.

fr.question: Faut-il remplacer la sonde aval en meme temps que la sonde amont ?
fr.answer: Pas obligatoirement, mais si les deux ont le meme kilometrage et que l'une lache, l'autre ne tardera pas. Remplacer les deux en meme temps evite de retourner au garage 6 mois plus tard.

en.question: What does the P0139 fault code mean?
en.answer: The P0139 code means your car's computer has detected a problem with downstream lambda sensor — slow response (bank 1). This is a low-severity emissions fault. Your mechanic can use an OBD2 diagnostic scanner to investigate further and identify the specific cause.

en.question: Can I drive with a P0139 code?
en.answer: Yes, you can generally continue driving with this code. It is a low-severity fault that should not cause immediate danger. However, you may notice some changes in performance or fuel consumption. Book a diagnostic appointment when convenient — the sooner you get it sorted, the less likely it is to cause further issues.

en.question: Will P0139 fail my MOT?
en.answer: Yes, this code is very likely to cause an MOT failure. Emissions are tested during the MOT, and a fault in the emissions system typically results in elevated exhaust gas readings that exceed the legal limits.

en.question: How much does P0139 repair cost in the UK?
en.answer: In the UK, repair costs for a P0139 fault typically range from around £68 to £219, depending on the root cause and your vehicle's make and model. The most common fix is ageing downstream lambda sensor, but a proper diagnostic is needed to confirm exactly what needs doing. Labour rates vary across the country, with London and the South East generally being more expensive than other regions.

motFail: Yes, this code is very likely to cause an MOT failure. Emissions are tested during the MOT, and a fault in the emissions system typically results in elevated exhaust gas readings that exceed the legal limits.

description.fr: Le P0140 est le degre le plus severe des codes de sonde aval : la sonde ne montre strictement aucune activite. Son signal reste completement fixe et ne varie pas du tout, quel que soit ce qui sort du catalyseur. La sonde est probablement completement morte ou son circuit est coupe. Comme pour les autres codes de sonde aval, l'impact sur la conduite est nul car cette sonde ne controle pas le melange. Mais le calculateur ne peut plus du tout surveiller le catalyseur, ce qui signifie qu'un probleme grave de catalyseur passerait completement inapercu. C'est aussi un voyant moteur permanent et un echec garanti au controle technique. Le remplacement est simple et abordable. Verifiez aussi le fusible du circuit chauffage des sondes qui est parfois commun aux sondes amont et aval.
description.en: The P0140 diagnostic trouble code indicates that your vehicle's emissions control system has flagged up an issue with downstream lambda sensor — no activity (bank 1). Your car's ECU constantly monitors exhaust gas composition using sensors before and after the catalytic converter. When the readings suggest the system is not cleaning up exhaust gases effectively, this fault code is stored. From a driving perspective, you may notice a slight drop in fuel economy or perhaps a rotten egg smell from the exhaust on occasion. The key concern here is the MOT test — emissions failures are one of the most common reasons cars fail, and this code directly relates to that. A trained technician can run a proper diagnostic to identify whether it is a sensor issue, a catalytic converter problem, or something simpler like an exhaust leak. Most of these repairs are straightforward and well within the capabilities of any decent garage. Getting it fixed promptly will keep your running costs down and ensure a smooth MOT experience.

fr.question: La voiture roule normalement avec un P0140, pourquoi s'en occuper ?
fr.answer: Parce que sans surveillance du catalyseur, un probleme grave (catalyseur bouche, surchauffe) ne sera pas detecte. De plus, le voyant moteur allume masque d'eventuels autres codes plus importants.

fr.question: Le fusible peut-il etre la seule cause ?
fr.answer: Oui, si le fusible alimente le chauffage de la sonde aval, la sonde ne chauffe pas et reste inactive. C'est la premiere chose a verifier car c'est la reparation la moins chere possible.

fr.question: Quand remplacer la sonde aval preventivellement ?
fr.answer: Autour de 120 000 a 150 000 km pour les sondes classiques. Les sondes a large bande peuvent durer plus longtemps. Si vous avez des codes P0139 ou P0133, c'est le signe que le remplacement approche.

en.question: What does the P0140 fault code mean?
en.answer: The P0140 code means your car's computer has detected a problem with downstream lambda sensor — no activity (bank 1). This is a low-severity emissions fault. Your mechanic can use an OBD2 diagnostic scanner to investigate further and identify the specific cause.

en.question: Can I drive with a P0140 code?
en.answer: Yes, you can generally continue driving with this code. It is a low-severity fault that should not cause immediate danger. However, you may notice some changes in performance or fuel consumption. Book a diagnostic appointment when convenient — the sooner you get it sorted, the less likely it is to cause further issues.

en.question: Will P0140 fail my MOT?
en.answer: Yes, this code is very likely to cause an MOT failure. Emissions are tested during the MOT, and a fault in the emissions system typically results in elevated exhaust gas readings that exceed the legal limits.

en.question: How much does P0140 repair cost in the UK?
en.answer: In the UK, repair costs for a P0140 fault typically range from around £4 to £219, depending on the root cause and your vehicle's make and model. The most common fix is completely dead downstream lambda sensor, but a proper diagnostic is needed to confirm exactly what needs doing. Labour rates vary across the country, with London and the South East generally being more expensive than other regions.

motFail: Yes, this code is very likely to cause an MOT failure. Emissions are tested during the MOT, and a fault in the emissions system typically results in elevated exhaust gas readings that exceed the legal limits.

name.fr: Capteur de pression de rampe carburant — circuit defaillant
name.en: Fuel rail pressure sensor — circuit fault

description.fr: Le P0190 concerne le capteur de pression de rampe commune (rail pressure sensor), principalement present sur les moteurs a injection directe essence et diesel common rail. Ce capteur mesure la pression du carburant dans la rampe d'injection, qui peut atteindre 200 a 2000 bars sur les diesels modernes. Le calculateur utilise cette information pour doser precisement l'injection. Si le signal est absent ou incoherent, le moteur peut passer en mode degrade, perdre beaucoup de puissance ou meme refuser de demarrer. Sur les diesels, ce capteur est critique car la pression de rail determine la qualite de la pulverisation et donc la combustion. Un capteur defaillant peut causer des rates d'allumage, des fumees et un manque de puissance important. Le remplacement necessite une cle dynamometrique car le capteur doit etre serre a un couple precis pour eviter les fuites de carburant haute pression.
description.en: When your car's computer stores a P0190 code, it is telling you there is a problem with fuel rail pressure sensor — circuit fault. This is an engine-related fault, and while it might sound alarming, many engine codes are more of a nuisance than an emergency. Your engine management system is incredibly sophisticated — it monitors hundreds of parameters every second to keep everything running smoothly. When something drifts outside the normal range, the system logs a fault code and illuminates the engine warning light on your dashboard. Depending on the severity, you might notice symptoms like rough running, hesitation during acceleration, slightly higher fuel consumption, or an unstable idle. In many cases, you can continue to drive carefully, but it is important to get the car checked as soon as reasonably possible. Ignoring engine faults can lead to secondary damage — for example, a misfire left unchecked can damage the catalytic converter over time. A good mechanic with proper diagnostic equipment will be able to read the code and the associated live data to identify the root cause and get you back on the road with confidence.

name.fr: Capteur de pression de rampe defaillant

fr.question: Ce code est-il specifique aux diesels ?
fr.answer: Non, il concerne aussi les moteurs essence a injection directe (GDI, FSI, TFSI, etc.). Mais il est plus courant sur les diesels common rail ou les pressions sont beaucoup plus elevees.

fr.question: La pompe haute pression peut-elle etre en cause ?
fr.answer: Oui, si la pression reelle dans la rampe est anormale, le capteur le detecte. Il faut differencier un capteur defaillant d'un vrai probleme de pression avec un outil de diagnostic professionnel.

fr.question: Le remplacement est-il complique ?
fr.answer: Le capteur lui-meme est facile a remplacer, mais sur les diesels haute pression il faut respecter le couple de serrage exact et utiliser un joint neuf pour eviter les fuites. Mieux vaut laisser faire un professionnel.

en.question: What does the P0190 fault code mean?
en.answer: The P0190 code means your car's computer has detected a problem with fuel rail pressure sensor — circuit fault. This is a high-severity engine fault. Your mechanic can use an OBD2 diagnostic scanner to investigate further and identify the specific cause.

en.question: Can I drive with a P0190 code?
en.answer: It is not recommended to drive with this fault code active. The issue it indicates could compromise your safety or cause serious damage to the vehicle. If possible, have the car recovered to a garage rather than driving it there.

en.question: Will P0190 fail my MOT?
en.answer: This code may cause an MOT failure if it results in excessive emissions or visible smoke. The engine warning light being illuminated is not an automatic MOT fail in the UK, but the underlying fault could affect emissions readings.

en.question: How much does P0190 repair cost in the UK?
en.answer: In the UK, repair costs for a P0190 fault typically range from around £25 to £1243, depending on the root cause and your vehicle's make and model. The most common fix is faulty fuel rail pressure sensor, but a proper diagnostic is needed to confirm exactly what needs doing. Labour rates vary across the country, with London and the South East generally being more expensive than other regions.

motFail: This code may cause an MOT failure if it results in excessive emissions or visible smoke. The engine warning light being illuminated is not an automatic MOT fail in the UK, but the underlying fault could affect emissions readings.

name.fr: Capteur de pression de rampe — plage/performance

description.fr: Le P0191 indique que le capteur de pression de rampe carburant envoie des valeurs qui ne correspondent pas a ce que le calculateur attend. Le signal est present mais incoherent avec les autres parametres : par exemple, la pression lue ne correspond pas au debit commande par le regulateur de pression. Ce code est souvent le signe que la pression reelle dans la rampe n'est pas celle attendue, ce qui peut venir d'un capteur defectueux, mais aussi d'un probleme mecanique reel. Une pompe haute pression fatiguee, un regulateur de pression bloque, un injecteur qui fuit en retour ou un filtre a carburant bouche peuvent tous produire ce code. Sur les diesels common rail, ce code peut s'accompagner de rates d'allumage, de fumees et d'une perte de puissance marquee. Le diagnostic necessite un outil capable de lire la pression de rampe en temps reel.
description.en: A P0191 fault code has been detected by your car's engine management system, indicating a problem with fuel rail pressure sensor — range/performance. The ECU is essentially the brain of your engine, and when it spots something out of the ordinary, it stores this code to help your mechanic diagnose the issue. You may have noticed the engine warning light on your dashboard, and perhaps some changes in how the car feels — maybe a slight judder at idle, a moment of hesitation when you press the accelerator, or a general feeling that the engine is not quite as responsive as usual. These are all typical signs of an engine management issue. The good news is that most of these problems have well-understood causes and straightforward fixes. It could be anything from a dirty sensor to a worn spark plug or a small air leak in the intake system. A proper OBD2 diagnostic at your local garage will identify exactly what is going on, and in many cases the repair is quicker and cheaper than you might fear. Do get it looked at sooner rather than later to avoid any knock-on problems.

name.fr: Capteur de pression de rampe defectueux
Regulateur de pression ou injecteur fuyant

fr.question: Le P0191 est-il toujours un probleme de capteur ?
fr.answer: Non, dans environ la moitie des cas c'est un probleme mecanique reel de pression de carburant. Le capteur peut etre bon mais detecter une anomalie reelle dans le circuit d'alimentation.

fr.question: Comment differencier un probleme de capteur d'un probleme de pompe ?
fr.answer: Avec un outil de diagnostic, comparez la pression commandee et la pression lue. Si elles divergent et que le capteur est bon (teste separement), le probleme est mecanique — pompe, regulateur ou injecteurs.

fr.question: Un filtre a carburant bouche peut-il causer ce code ?
fr.answer: Oui, surtout sur les diesels. Un filtre bouche reduit le debit vers la pompe haute pression, qui ne peut plus atteindre la pression demandee. C'est la verification la plus simple et la moins chere a faire.

en.question: What does the P0191 fault code mean?
en.answer: The P0191 code means your car's computer has detected a problem with fuel rail pressure sensor — range/performance. This is a high-severity engine fault. Your mechanic can use an OBD2 diagnostic scanner to investigate further and identify the specific cause.

en.question: Can I drive with a P0191 code?
en.answer: You can drive short distances with this code, but it is advisable to get it checked soon. This is a high-severity fault that could worsen over time. Avoid long motorway journeys until a mechanic has had a look, and keep an eye on your temperature gauge and warning lights.

en.question: Will P0191 fail my MOT?
en.answer: This code may cause an MOT failure if it results in excessive emissions or visible smoke. The engine warning light being illuminated is not an automatic MOT fail in the UK, but the underlying fault could affect emissions readings.

en.question: How much does P0191 repair cost in the UK?
en.answer: In the UK, repair costs for a P0191 fault typically range from around £70 to £1271, depending on the root cause and your vehicle's make and model. The most common fix is worn high-pressure fuel pump, but a proper diagnostic is needed to confirm exactly what needs doing. Labour rates vary across the country, with London and the South East generally being more expensive than other regions.

motFail: This code may cause an MOT failure if it results in excessive emissions or visible smoke. The engine warning light being illuminated is not an automatic MOT fail in the UK, but the underlying fault could affect emissions readings.

name.fr: Capteur de pression de rampe carburant — tension basse
name.en: Fuel rail pressure sensor — low voltage

description.fr: Le P0192 est specifique : la tension electrique du capteur de pression de rampe est anormalement basse. Cela indique generalement un court-circuit dans le cablage du capteur ou un capteur defaillant dont la sortie est en court-circuit a la masse. Le calculateur interprete cette tension basse comme une pression de carburant tres faible, ce qui peut declencher un mode degrade ou meme l'arret du moteur par securite. Sur les diesels common rail, une pression de rampe trop basse peut endommager les injecteurs et la pompe si le moteur continue de tourner. Verifiez en priorite le connecteur du capteur et le cablage. Un fil pince, un connecteur qui a pris l'eau ou un capteur dont le joint est deteriore sont les causes electriques les plus frequentes. Si le cablage est bon, remplacez le capteur.
description.en: The diagnostic trouble code P0192 relates to fuel rail pressure sensor — low voltage, which is a fault within the engine management system. Your car uses dozens of sensors and actuators to ensure the engine runs efficiently, and when one of these components starts to play up, the ECU stores a fault code and turns on the engine warning light. This is your car's way of asking for a bit of attention. Common symptoms you might experience include rough idling, slight misfires, reduced acceleration, or increased fuel consumption. While many engine fault codes do not require you to stop driving immediately, it is always wise to get the issue investigated promptly. A trained technician will connect a diagnostic tool to your car's OBD2 port, read the code, and examine the live data to determine the exact cause. Repairs vary from simple maintenance tasks like replacing spark plugs or cleaning sensors, to more involved work on components like the throttle body or fuel injectors. Whatever the cause, addressing it early will save you money in the long run and keep your car running at its best.

fr.question: Mon diesel s'est arrete et ne redemarre pas, est-ce le P0192 ?
fr.answer: C'est possible. Si le calculateur lit une pression de rampe a zero, il coupe l'injection par securite. Verifiez le connecteur du capteur — un simple debranchement accidentel peut provoquer ce scenario.

fr.question: Un lavage haute pression peut-il causer ce code ?
fr.answer: Oui, un jet d'eau haute pression sur le moteur peut infiltrer le connecteur du capteur et creer un court-circuit temporaire ou permanent. Protegez les connecteurs avant tout lavage moteur.

fr.question: Peut-on demarrer en rebranchant simplement le connecteur ?
fr.answer: Si le connecteur etait debranche ou mouille, oui. Sechez-le, rebranchz-le et effacez les codes. Si le probleme revient, le capteur ou le cablage est endommage en profondeur.

en.question: What does the P0192 fault code mean?
en.answer: The P0192 code means your car's computer has detected a problem with fuel rail pressure sensor — low voltage. This is a high-severity engine fault. Your mechanic can use an OBD2 diagnostic scanner to investigate further and identify the specific cause.

en.question: Can I drive with a P0192 code?
en.answer: It is not recommended to drive with this fault code active. The issue it indicates could compromise your safety or cause serious damage to the vehicle. If possible, have the car recovered to a garage rather than driving it there.

en.question: Will P0192 fail my MOT?
en.answer: This code may cause an MOT failure if it results in excessive emissions or visible smoke. The engine warning light being illuminated is not an automatic MOT fail in the UK, but the underlying fault could affect emissions readings.

en.question: How much does P0192 repair cost in the UK?
en.answer: In the UK, repair costs for a P0192 fault typically range from around £12 to £257, depending on the root cause and your vehicle's make and model. The most common fix is pressure sensor short circuit, but a proper diagnostic is needed to confirm exactly what needs doing. Labour rates vary across the country, with London and the South East generally being more expensive than other regions.

motFail: This code may cause an MOT failure if it results in excessive emissions or visible smoke. The engine warning light being illuminated is not an automatic MOT fail in the UK, but the underlying fault could affect emissions readings.

name.fr: Capteur de pression de rampe carburant — tension haute
name.en: Fuel rail pressure sensor — high voltage

description.fr: Le P0193 est l'oppose du P0192 : la tension du capteur de pression de rampe est trop haute. Le calculateur interprete cela comme une pression de carburant extremement elevee, ce qui est dangereux car une surpression dans la rampe peut endommager les injecteurs, la rampe elle-meme ou creer des fuites de carburant haute pression. Le calculateur reagit en mode degrade pour tenter de reduire la pression. Cependant, si c'est un faux signal du capteur, la pression reelle est peut-etre normale et les mesures prises par le calculateur perturbent inutilement le fonctionnement. Un fil ouvert ou un capteur dont le signal de reference est perturbe sont les causes electriques les plus courantes. Si la pression est reellement haute, il faut chercher du cote du regulateur de pression bloque ou d'un retour injecteur bouche.
description.en: When your car's computer stores a P0193 code, it is telling you there is a problem with fuel rail pressure sensor — high voltage. This is an engine-related fault, and while it might sound alarming, many engine codes are more of a nuisance than an emergency. Your engine management system is incredibly sophisticated — it monitors hundreds of parameters every second to keep everything running smoothly. When something drifts outside the normal range, the system logs a fault code and illuminates the engine warning light on your dashboard. Depending on the severity, you might notice symptoms like rough running, hesitation during acceleration, slightly higher fuel consumption, or an unstable idle. In many cases, you can continue to drive carefully, but it is important to get the car checked as soon as reasonably possible. Ignoring engine faults can lead to secondary damage — for example, a misfire left unchecked can damage the catalytic converter over time. A good mechanic with proper diagnostic equipment will be able to read the code and the associated live data to identify the root cause and get you back on the road with confidence.

name.fr: Capteur de pression en circuit ouvert

fr.question: Une pression de rampe trop haute est-elle dangereuse ?
fr.answer: Oui, potentiellement. Sur les diesels, les pressions atteignent 1500-2000 bars. Une surpression peut endommager les injecteurs, la rampe ou creer une fuite de carburant a haute pression, ce qui est un risque d'incendie.

fr.question: Le capteur peut-il donner un faux signal de haute pression ?
fr.answer: Oui, un circuit ouvert dans le cablage du capteur produit une tension maximale que le calculateur interprete comme une pression tres elevee. Le diagnostic doit differencier un faux signal d'une vraie surpression.

fr.question: Comment fonctionne le regulateur de pression de rampe ?
fr.answer: Le regulateur est une electrovanne qui laisse echapper du carburant de la rampe vers le reservoir pour maintenir la bonne pression. S'il se bloque ferme, la pression monte au-dessus de la consigne.

en.question: What does the P0193 fault code mean?
en.answer: The P0193 code means your car's computer has detected a problem with fuel rail pressure sensor — high voltage. This is a high-severity engine fault. Your mechanic can use an OBD2 diagnostic scanner to investigate further and identify the specific cause.

en.question: Can I drive with a P0193 code?
en.answer: It is not recommended to drive with this fault code active. The issue it indicates could compromise your safety or cause serious damage to the vehicle. If possible, have the car recovered to a garage rather than driving it there.

en.question: Will P0193 fail my MOT?
en.answer: This code may cause an MOT failure if it results in excessive emissions or visible smoke. The engine warning light being illuminated is not an automatic MOT fail in the UK, but the underlying fault could affect emissions readings.

en.question: How much does P0193 repair cost in the UK?
en.answer: In the UK, repair costs for a P0193 fault typically range from around £69 to £425, depending on the root cause and your vehicle's make and model. The most common fix is pressure sensor open circuit, but a proper diagnostic is needed to confirm exactly what needs doing. Labour rates vary across the country, with London and the South East generally being more expensive than other regions.

motFail: This code may cause an MOT failure if it results in excessive emissions or visible smoke. The engine warning light being illuminated is not an automatic MOT fail in the UK, but the underlying fault could affect emissions readings.

description.fr: Le code P0200 est un code generique qui signale un probleme sur le circuit electrique des injecteurs sans preciser lequel est en cause. Le calculateur a detecte une anomalie dans le systeme d'alimentation des injecteurs : cela peut etre un probleme de cablage, un driver d'injecteur defaillant dans le calculateur, ou un injecteur lui-meme en court-circuit. Ce code est souvent accompagne de codes specifiques par cylindre (P0201 a P0204) qui aident a localiser le probleme. Si le P0200 apparait seul, c'est souvent un probleme commun a plusieurs injecteurs, comme un faisceau endommage ou un relais d'alimentation defaillant. Les symptomes varient selon la gravite : d'un simple rate d'allumage a un moteur qui ne demarre pas du tout. Le diagnostic electrique du faisceau d'injection est la premiere etape pour identifier la cause.
description.en: A P0200 fault code has been detected by your car's engine management system, indicating a problem with fuel injector circuit — malfunction. The ECU is essentially the brain of your engine, and when it spots something out of the ordinary, it stores this code to help your mechanic diagnose the issue. You may have noticed the engine warning light on your dashboard, and perhaps some changes in how the car feels — maybe a slight judder at idle, a moment of hesitation when you press the accelerator, or a general feeling that the engine is not quite as responsive as usual. These are all typical signs of an engine management issue. The good news is that most of these problems have well-understood causes and straightforward fixes. It could be anything from a dirty sensor to a worn spark plug or a small air leak in the intake system. A proper OBD2 diagnostic at your local garage will identify exactly what is going on, and in many cases the repair is quicker and cheaper than you might fear. Do get it looked at sooner rather than later to avoid any knock-on problems.

fr.question: Le P0200 indique-t-il quel injecteur est en panne ?
fr.answer: Non, c'est un code generique. Les codes P0201 a P0204 (et au-dela) specifient quel cylindre est concerne. Si seul le P0200 apparait, le probleme est probablement commun a tous les injecteurs.

fr.question: Un relais d'injecteurs, c'est quoi ?
fr.answer: C'est un interrupteur electrique qui alimente en courant tous les injecteurs. S'il tombe en panne, aucun injecteur ne recoit de courant et le moteur ne demarre pas. C'est une piece a 20-40 euros.

fr.question: Comment tester un injecteur electriquement ?
fr.answer: Mesurez la resistance de l'injecteur avec un multimetre. Elle doit etre entre 10 et 16 ohms pour la plupart des injecteurs. Un court-circuit (0 ohm) ou un circuit ouvert (infini) confirme un injecteur HS.

en.question: What does the P0200 fault code mean?
en.answer: The P0200 code means your car's computer has detected a problem with fuel injector circuit — malfunction. This is a high-severity engine fault. Your mechanic can use an OBD2 diagnostic scanner to investigate further and identify the specific cause.

en.question: Can I drive with a P0200 code?
en.answer: You can drive short distances with this code, but it is advisable to get it checked soon. This is a high-severity fault that could worsen over time. Avoid long motorway journeys until a mechanic has had a look, and keep an eye on your temperature gauge and warning lights.

en.question: Will P0200 fail my MOT?
en.answer: This code may cause an MOT failure if it results in excessive emissions or visible smoke. The engine warning light being illuminated is not an automatic MOT fail in the UK, but the underlying fault could affect emissions readings.

en.question: How much does P0200 repair cost in the UK?
en.answer: In the UK, repair costs for a P0200 fault typically range from around £25 to £339, depending on the root cause and your vehicle's make and model. The most common fix is damaged injection wiring harness, but a proper diagnostic is needed to confirm exactly what needs doing. Labour rates vary across the country, with London and the South East generally being more expensive than other regions.

motFail: This code may cause an MOT failure if it results in excessive emissions or visible smoke. The engine warning light being illuminated is not an automatic MOT fail in the UK, but the underlying fault could affect emissions readings.

description.fr: Le P0201 pointe specifiquement un probleme electrique sur l'injecteur du cylindre 1. Le calculateur a detecte que le circuit de commande de cet injecteur ne fonctionne pas normalement : la resistance est hors plage, le courant consomme est anormal ou le signal de retour est absent. L'injecteur peut etre en circuit ouvert, en court-circuit, ou son connecteur peut etre defaillant. Un injecteur qui ne s'ouvre pas provoque un rate d'allumage sur le cylindre 1 (P0301 associe) car aucun carburant n'est injecte. Un injecteur en court-circuit peut surcharger le driver du calculateur et potentiellement l'endommager. Verifiez d'abord le connecteur de l'injecteur et permutez-le avec un autre cylindre pour confirmer le diagnostic avant de remplacer l'injecteur.
description.en: The diagnostic trouble code P0201 relates to fuel injector cylinder 1 — circuit fault, which is a fault within the engine management system. Your car uses dozens of sensors and actuators to ensure the engine runs efficiently, and when one of these components starts to play up, the ECU stores a fault code and turns on the engine warning light. This is your car's way of asking for a bit of attention. Common symptoms you might experience include rough idling, slight misfires, reduced acceleration, or increased fuel consumption. While many engine fault codes do not require you to stop driving immediately, it is always wise to get the issue investigated promptly. A trained technician will connect a diagnostic tool to your car's OBD2 port, read the code, and examine the live data to determine the exact cause. Repairs vary from simple maintenance tasks like replacing spark plugs or cleaning sensors, to more involved work on components like the throttle body or fuel injectors. Whatever the cause, addressing it early will save you money in the long run and keep your car running at its best.

name.en: Wiring of the wiring harness of injection

en: Smell of petrol si fuel injector fuit

fr.question: Comment permuter les injecteurs pour diagnostic ?
fr.answer: Echangez l'injecteur du cylindre 1 avec celui du cylindre 2. Si le code passe de P0201 a P0202, c'est l'injecteur. Si le P0201 reste, c'est le cablage ou le connecteur du cylindre 1.

fr.question: Un injecteur peut-il endommager le calculateur ?
fr.answer: Un injecteur en court-circuit peut effectivement surcharger le driver de sortie du calculateur. C'est rare mais possible. Remplacez un injecteur en court-circuit avant de remettre le moteur en route.

fr.question: Les injecteurs diesel sont-ils plus chers ?
fr.answer: Oui, significativement. Un injecteur common rail diesel coute entre 200 et 600 euros la piece, contre 80 a 200 euros pour un injecteur essence. Les injecteurs piezo sont les plus chers.

en.question: What does the P0201 fault code mean?
en.answer: The P0201 code means your car's computer has detected a problem with fuel injector cylinder 1 — circuit fault. This is a medium-severity engine fault. Your mechanic can use an OBD2 diagnostic scanner to investigate further and identify the specific cause.

en.question: Can I drive with a P0201 code?
en.answer: Yes, you can generally continue driving with this code. It is a medium-severity fault that should not cause immediate danger. However, you may notice some changes in performance or fuel consumption. Book a diagnostic appointment when convenient — the sooner you get it sorted, the less likely it is to cause further issues.

en.question: Will P0201 fail my MOT?
en.answer: The engine warning light alone does not cause an automatic MOT failure in the UK. However, if this fault affects exhaust emissions or causes visible smoke, it could result in a fail on the emissions test.

en.question: How much does P0201 repair cost in the UK?
en.answer: In the UK, repair costs for a P0201 fault typically range from around £13 to £290, depending on the root cause and your vehicle's make and model. The most common fix is fuel injector cylinder 1 faulty, but a proper diagnostic is needed to confirm exactly what needs doing. Labour rates vary across the country, with London and the South East generally being more expensive than other regions.

motFail: The engine warning light alone does not cause an automatic MOT failure in the UK. However, if this fault affects exhaust emissions or causes visible smoke, it could result in a fail on the emissions test.

description.fr: Le P0202 designe un defaut electrique sur le circuit de l'injecteur du cylindre 2. Le diagnostic et les causes sont identiques au P0201 mais localises sur le deuxieme cylindre. Le connecteur de l'injecteur du cylindre 2 peut etre plus expose a l'humidite ou aux eclaboussures sur certaines configurations moteur, ce qui favorise la corrosion. Un injecteur qui colle en position ouverte sur un seul cylindre provoque une surconsommation localisee, un lavage des cylindres (l'essence nettoie l'huile des parois) et a terme des degats sur les segments et les pistons. Si l'injecteur refuse de s'ouvrir, le cylindre ne produit aucune puissance et le moteur tourne de maniere desequilibree. La permutation d'injecteurs entre cylindres reste la methode de diagnostic la plus fiable avant de proceder au remplacement.
description.en: When your car's computer stores a P0202 code, it is telling you there is a problem with fuel injector cylinder 2 — circuit fault. This is an engine-related fault, and while it might sound alarming, many engine codes are more of a nuisance than an emergency. Your engine management system is incredibly sophisticated — it monitors hundreds of parameters every second to keep everything running smoothly. When something drifts outside the normal range, the system logs a fault code and illuminates the engine warning light on your dashboard. Depending on the severity, you might notice symptoms like rough running, hesitation during acceleration, slightly higher fuel consumption, or an unstable idle. In many cases, you can continue to drive carefully, but it is important to get the car checked as soon as reasonably possible. Ignoring engine faults can lead to secondary damage — for example, a misfire left unchecked can damage the catalytic converter over time. A good mechanic with proper diagnostic equipment will be able to read the code and the associated live data to identify the root cause and get you back on the road with confidence.

name.fr: Fil du faisceau coupe ou pince
name.en: Cut or pinched wire in harness

fr.question: Un injecteur qui colle ouvert, c'est grave ?
fr.answer: Oui, le carburant injecte en continu lave les parois du cylindre, detruisant le film d'huile protecteur. A la longue, les segments s'usent prematuremment et le moteur consomme de l'huile.

fr.question: Peut-on rouler avec un injecteur defaillant ?
fr.answer: Sur un court trajet oui, mais le moteur tourne de maniere desequilibree et le catalyseur souffre. Plus vous attendez, plus la reparation risque de s'alourdir.

fr.question: Le nettoyage d'injecteurs par ultrasons fonctionne-t-il ?
fr.answer: Pour un injecteur encrasse, oui. Le nettoyage aux ultrasons est tres efficace pour retirer les depots. Mais pour un injecteur avec un defaut electrique interne, seul le remplacement est possible.

en.question: What does the P0202 fault code mean?

en.question: Can I drive with a P0202 code?
