motFail: The engine warning light alone does not cause an automatic MOT failure in the UK. However, if this fault affects exhaust emissions or causes visible smoke, it could result in a fail on the emissions test.

description.fr: Le P0562 indique que le calculateur moteur a mesure une tension d'alimentation inferieure a la normale, generalement en dessous de 12 volts moteur tournant. L'alternateur devrait fournir entre 13.5V et 14.5V quand le moteur tourne. Une tension basse signifie que la batterie se decharge plus vite qu'elle ne se charge. Les causes incluent un alternateur fatigue dont les diodes ou les balais sont uses, une courroie d'accessoires patinante, une batterie en fin de vie qui ne tient plus la charge, ou un consommateur electrique parasite qui vide la batterie. Ce code est a prendre au serieux car une tension trop basse peut causer des dysfonctionnements de tous les calculateurs du vehicule et mener a une panne complete.
description.en: The P0562 code indicates an electrical system fault related to system voltage — low. Modern cars rely heavily on electrical components, and when there is an issue with the voltage supply, a sensor circuit, or a control module, the ECU logs a fault code to flag the problem. Electrical faults can be tricky because they sometimes cause unexpected symptoms — you might notice flickering dashboard lights, erratic instrument readings, a battery that drains overnight, or even problems starting the engine. In many cases, the root cause is something straightforward like a corroded connector, a blown fuse, or a weak battery approaching end of life. However, electrical issues can also indicate more serious problems with the alternator, the wiring harness, or even the ECU itself. A mechanic with proper diagnostic equipment can trace the electrical fault systematically, checking voltages and continuity to find the source. Do not attempt to ignore electrical faults, as they can lead to bigger problems if left untreated — for instance, an undercharging alternator will eventually leave you stranded with a flat battery.

fr.question: Mon voyant batterie s'allume en roulant, est-ce grave ?
fr.answer: Oui, cela signifie que l'alternateur ne charge plus correctement. Vous roulez sur la reserve de la batterie. Selon son etat, vous avez entre 30 minutes et 2 heures avant la panne complete.

fr.question: Comment tester l'alternateur ?
fr.answer: Avec un multimetre aux bornes de la batterie moteur tournant : 13.5V a 14.5V = alternateur OK. En dessous de 13V = alternateur faible. Au-dessus de 15V = regulateur defaillant.

fr.question: Une batterie neuve peut-elle resoudre le P0562 ?
fr.answer: Si le probleme est la batterie elle-meme, oui. Mais si l'alternateur est faible, la nouvelle batterie sera a son tour decharge en quelques jours. Il faut traiter la cause racine.

en.question: What does the P0562 fault code mean?
en.answer: The P0562 code means your car's computer has detected a problem with system voltage — low. This is a medium-severity electrical fault. Your mechanic can use an OBD2 diagnostic scanner to investigate further and identify the specific cause.

en.question: Can I drive with a P0562 code?
en.answer: Yes, you can generally continue driving with this code. It is a medium-severity fault that should not cause immediate danger. However, you may notice some changes in performance or fuel consumption. Book a diagnostic appointment when convenient — the sooner you get it sorted, the less likely it is to cause further issues.

en.question: Will P0562 fail my MOT?
en.answer: An illuminated engine management light is not an automatic MOT failure. However, if the electrical fault affects lighting, emissions, or other testable systems, it could contribute to a fail.

en.question: How much does P0562 repair cost in the UK?
en.answer: In the UK, repair costs for a P0562 fault typically range from around £26 to £527, depending on the root cause and your vehicle's make and model. The most common fix is faulty alternator, but a proper diagnostic is needed to confirm exactly what needs doing. Labour rates vary across the country, with London and the South East generally being more expensive than other regions.

motFail: An illuminated engine management light is not an automatic MOT failure. However, if the electrical fault affects lighting, emissions, or other testable systems, it could contribute to a fail.

description.fr: Le P0563 est l'inverse du P0562 : la tension du systeme est trop elevee, generalement au-dessus de 15 volts. Le regulateur de l'alternateur est probablement defaillant et laisse passer trop de courant. Une surtension chronique endommage les ampoules, les calculateurs et la batterie. Les ampoules grillent plus souvent que la normale et la batterie gonfle et se deteriore. C'est un probleme a corriger rapidement car les consequences sur l'electronique sont couteuses. Le regulateur est parfois integre a l'alternateur et necessite le remplacement de l'ensemble. Sur certains vehicules, un probleme de masse (fil de masse corrode ou deconnecte) peut aussi causer une surtension apparente.
description.en: Your car has stored a P0563 fault code, which points to an electrical issue with system voltage — high. The electrical system in a modern vehicle is remarkably complex, with multiple computers communicating over high-speed data networks and dozens of sensors feeding information back to the engine management system. When something goes wrong in this electrical web, the symptoms can be varied and sometimes puzzling. You might see warning lights appearing on the dashboard, experience intermittent starting problems, or notice that certain features like the radio or instrument cluster behave strangely. Common causes include ageing batteries, corroded earth connections, damaged wiring, or faulty relays and fuses. An auto electrician or a well-equipped garage can diagnose these issues using a combination of OBD2 scanners and multimeters. The repair could be as simple as cleaning a connection or replacing a fuse, or it might require more in-depth work on the alternator or a control module. Either way, electrical faults tend to get worse over time, so it is best to get them sorted sooner rather than later to avoid an unexpected breakdown.

fr.question: Une surtension peut-elle griller mon calculateur ?
fr.answer: Oui, une tension superieure a 16V pendant une periode prolongee peut endommager les composants electroniques sensibles. Corrigez le probleme rapidement.

fr.question: Comment verifier si c'est le regulateur ?
fr.answer: Mesurez la tension aux bornes de la batterie moteur tournant. Si elle depasse 14.8V et monte progressivement, le regulateur est defaillant. A plus de 15.5V, coupez le moteur.

fr.question: La batterie est-elle endommagee ?
fr.answer: Une surtension prolongee fait bouillir l'electrolyte de la batterie, ce qui la deteriore. Si la batterie gonfle ou sent l'oeuf pourri (acide), elle est probablement endommagee et doit etre remplacee.

en.question: What does the P0563 fault code mean?
en.answer: The P0563 code means your car's computer has detected a problem with system voltage — high. This is a high-severity electrical fault. Your mechanic can use an OBD2 diagnostic scanner to investigate further and identify the specific cause.

en.question: Can I drive with a P0563 code?
en.answer: You can drive short distances with this code, but it is advisable to get it checked soon. This is a high-severity fault that could worsen over time. Avoid long motorway journeys until a mechanic has had a look, and keep an eye on your temperature gauge and warning lights.

en.question: Will P0563 fail my MOT?
en.answer: An illuminated engine management light is not an automatic MOT failure. However, if the electrical fault affects lighting, emissions, or other testable systems, it could contribute to a fail.

en.question: How much does P0563 repair cost in the UK?
en.answer: In the UK, repair costs for a P0563 fault typically range from around £17 to £524, depending on the root cause and your vehicle's make and model. The most common fix is faulty alternator voltage regulator, but a proper diagnostic is needed to confirm exactly what needs doing. Labour rates vary across the country, with London and the South East generally being more expensive than other regions.

motFail: An illuminated engine management light is not an automatic MOT failure. However, if the electrical fault affects lighting, emissions, or other testable systems, it could contribute to a fail.

name.fr: Liaison serie — bus CAN interne calculateur
name.en: Serial communication link — internal ECU CAN bus

description.fr: Le P0600 signale un probleme de communication interne au calculateur moteur. Le processeur principal n'arrive plus a communiquer correctement avec les sous-systemes internes via le bus serie. C'est un code rare qui pointe souvent vers un probleme de calculateur lui-meme : memoire corrompue, composant interne defaillant ou alimentation instable. Avant de conclure que le calculateur est HS, verifiez les alimentations et les masses du calculateur car une tension instable peut provoquer des erreurs de communication internes. Un contact oxyde sur le connecteur du calculateur ou une mise a jour logicielle corrompue sont aussi des pistes. Ce code est plus frequent apres un debranchement de batterie mal gere ou une tentative de reprogrammation avortee.
description.en: The P0600 code indicates an electrical system fault related to serial communication link — internal ecu can bus. Modern cars rely heavily on electrical components, and when there is an issue with the voltage supply, a sensor circuit, or a control module, the ECU logs a fault code to flag the problem. Electrical faults can be tricky because they sometimes cause unexpected symptoms — you might notice flickering dashboard lights, erratic instrument readings, a battery that drains overnight, or even problems starting the engine. In many cases, the root cause is something straightforward like a corroded connector, a blown fuse, or a weak battery approaching end of life. However, electrical issues can also indicate more serious problems with the alternator, the wiring harness, or even the ECU itself. A mechanic with proper diagnostic equipment can trace the electrical fault systematically, checking voltages and continuity to find the source. Do not attempt to ignore electrical faults, as they can lead to bigger problems if left untreated — for instance, an undercharging alternator will eventually leave you stranded with a flat battery.

fr.question: Le calculateur est-il forcement mort ?
fr.answer: Pas toujours. Verifiez d'abord les connecteurs et les alimentations. Un probleme de masse ou de tension instable peut causer ce code sans que le calculateur soit HS.

fr.question: Peut-on reparer un calculateur ?
fr.answer: Oui, des specialistes en electronique automobile peuvent reparer et reprogrammer les calculateurs. C'est souvent 50 a 70% moins cher qu'un remplacement neuf.

fr.question: Une mise a jour logicielle peut-elle causer ce code ?
fr.answer: Oui, une reprogrammation interrompue ou echouee peut corrompre le firmware du calculateur. Un passage chez le concessionnaire pour reflasher le calculateur peut resoudre le probleme.

en.question: What does the P0600 fault code mean?
en.answer: The P0600 code means your car's computer has detected a problem with serial communication link — internal ecu can bus. This is a high-severity electrical fault. Your mechanic can use an OBD2 diagnostic scanner to investigate further and identify the specific cause.

en.question: Can I drive with a P0600 code?
en.answer: It is not recommended to drive with this fault code active. The issue it indicates could compromise your safety or cause serious damage to the vehicle. If possible, have the car recovered to a garage rather than driving it there.

en.question: Will P0600 fail my MOT?
en.answer: An illuminated engine management light is not an automatic MOT failure. However, if the electrical fault affects lighting, emissions, or other testable systems, it could contribute to a fail.

en.question: How much does P0600 repair cost in the UK?
en.answer: In the UK, repair costs for a P0600 fault typically range from around £25 to £1720, depending on the root cause and your vehicle's make and model. The most common fix is faulty engine ecu, but a proper diagnostic is needed to confirm exactly what needs doing. Labour rates vary across the country, with London and the South East generally being more expensive than other regions.

motFail: An illuminated engine management light is not an automatic MOT failure. However, if the electrical fault affects lighting, emissions, or other testable systems, it could contribute to a fail.

description.fr: Le P0601 indique que le calculateur moteur a detecte une erreur dans sa memoire interne, typiquement un echec de la verification par somme de controle (checksum). Cela signifie que les donnees stockees dans la memoire du calculateur ne sont plus integres. Les causes peuvent etre une surtension qui a endommage les puces memoire, une tentative de reprogrammation avortee, ou simplement la degradation naturelle des composants electroniques apres de nombreuses annees. Les consequences vont d'un simple voyant moteur a un comportement totalement imprevisible du moteur. C'est un code serieux qui necessite generalement une intervention chez le concessionnaire ou un specialiste electronique pour reflasher ou remplacer le calculateur.
description.en: Your car has stored a P0601 fault code, which points to an electrical issue with engine ecu — memory error. The electrical system in a modern vehicle is remarkably complex, with multiple computers communicating over high-speed data networks and dozens of sensors feeding information back to the engine management system. When something goes wrong in this electrical web, the symptoms can be varied and sometimes puzzling. You might see warning lights appearing on the dashboard, experience intermittent starting problems, or notice that certain features like the radio or instrument cluster behave strangely. Common causes include ageing batteries, corroded earth connections, damaged wiring, or faulty relays and fuses. An auto electrician or a well-equipped garage can diagnose these issues using a combination of OBD2 scanners and multimeters. The repair could be as simple as cleaning a connection or replacing a fuse, or it might require more in-depth work on the alternator or a control module. Either way, electrical faults tend to get worse over time, so it is best to get them sorted sooner rather than later to avoid an unexpected breakdown.

fr.question: La memoire du calculateur peut-elle se corrompre toute seule ?
fr.answer: Avec le temps et les cycles thermiques, les composants electroniques se degradent. C'est rare mais possible apres 10-15 ans. Les surtensions accelerent ce processus.

fr.question: Un reflashage suffit-il ?
fr.answer: Si la memoire flash est intacte et que seules les donnees sont corrompues, un reflashage chez le concessionnaire peut resoudre le probleme. Si la puce memoire est physiquement endommagee, il faut remplacer.

fr.question: Combien coute un calculateur neuf ?
fr.answer: Entre 500 et 2000 euros selon la marque et le modele. Un calculateur reconditionne ou repare revient a 200-800 euros. La reprogrammation est necessaire dans tous les cas.

en.question: What does the P0601 fault code mean?
en.answer: The P0601 code means your car's computer has detected a problem with engine ecu — memory error. This is a critical-severity electrical fault. Your mechanic can use an OBD2 diagnostic scanner to investigate further and identify the specific cause.

en.question: Can I drive with a P0601 code?
en.answer: It is not recommended to drive with this fault code active. The issue it indicates could compromise your safety or cause serious damage to the vehicle. If possible, have the car recovered to a garage rather than driving it there.

en.question: Will P0601 fail my MOT?
en.answer: An illuminated engine management light is not an automatic MOT failure. However, if the electrical fault affects lighting, emissions, or other testable systems, it could contribute to a fail.

en.question: How much does P0601 repair cost in the UK?
en.answer: In the UK, repair costs for a P0601 fault typically range from around £87 to £1685, depending on the root cause and your vehicle's make and model. The most common fix is ecu corrupted memory, but a proper diagnostic is needed to confirm exactly what needs doing. Labour rates vary across the country, with London and the South East generally being more expensive than other regions.

motFail: An illuminated engine management light is not an automatic MOT failure. However, if the electrical fault affects lighting, emissions, or other testable systems, it could contribute to a fail.

name.fr: Systeme de transmission — code defaut present

description.fr: Le P0700 est un code informatif du calculateur moteur qui indique qu'un defaut a ete enregistre dans le calculateur de la transmission automatique. Ce n'est pas un diagnostic en soi mais un signal d'alerte : il faut lire les codes du calculateur de transmission pour connaitre la nature exacte du probleme. La plupart des outils OBD2 basiques ne lisent que le calculateur moteur, qui signale le P0700 pour dire qu'il y a quelque chose a voir du cote de la boite. Un outil capable de lire les codes de transmission specifiques est necessaire. Les problemes peuvent aller d'un simple capteur de vitesse a un defaut mecanique de la boite elle-meme.
description.en: The P0700 diagnostic code indicates a problem with gearbox system — fault code present in your gearbox system. Modern automatic and semi-automatic gearboxes are controlled by a dedicated computer that manages gear changes, torque converter operation, and various solenoid valves. When this computer detects an anomaly, it stores a fault code and may put the gearbox into limp mode — this is a safety feature that limits the car to one or two gears to protect the transmission from further damage. You might notice harsh or delayed gear changes, a reluctance to shift, or the car staying in a low gear regardless of speed. While it can be concerning, limp mode is actually your gearbox protecting itself. A specialist garage with gearbox diagnostic equipment can read the code and assess whether the issue is electrical — such as a faulty speed sensor or solenoid — or mechanical, like worn clutch packs or a failing torque converter. Gearbox repairs can be costly, so early diagnosis is essential. Sometimes a simple fluid change and solenoid replacement is all that is needed, saving you from a far more expensive rebuild.

fr.question: Le P0700 me dit-il ce qui ne va pas avec ma boite ?
fr.answer: Non, le P0700 dit simplement qu'il y a un code dans le calculateur de transmission. Il faut un scanner capable de lire les codes transmission pour connaitre le probleme exact.

fr.question: Ma boite est-elle en danger avec un P0700 ?
fr.answer: Pas necessairement. Ca peut etre un simple capteur. Mais ne tardez pas a diagnostiquer car certains problemes de transmission s'aggravent rapidement si on continue a rouler.

fr.question: Un outil OBD2 basique peut-il lire les codes transmission ?
fr.answer: La plupart des outils basiques ne lisent que le calculateur moteur (Mode 03). Il faut un outil capable de lire les modules supplementaires ou un scanner constructeur specifique.

en.question: What does the P0700 fault code mean?
en.answer: The P0700 code means your car's computer has detected a problem with gearbox system — fault code present. This is a medium-severity gearbox fault. Your mechanic can use an OBD2 diagnostic scanner to investigate further and identify the specific cause.

en.question: Can I drive with a P0700 code?
en.answer: Yes, you can generally continue driving with this code. It is a medium-severity fault that should not cause immediate danger. However, you may notice some changes in performance or fuel consumption. Book a diagnostic appointment when convenient — the sooner you get it sorted, the less likely it is to cause further issues.

en.question: Will P0700 fail my MOT?
en.answer: Gearbox faults do not typically cause a direct MOT failure unless they affect the operation of the vehicle in a way that makes it unsafe. However, associated warning lights or driveability issues may be noted.

en.question: How much does P0700 repair cost in the UK?
en.answer: In the UK, repair costs for a P0700 fault typically range from around £44 to £2564, depending on the root cause and your vehicle's make and model. The most common fix is gearbox diagnostic required, but a proper diagnostic is needed to confirm exactly what needs doing. Labour rates vary across the country, with London and the South East generally being more expensive than other regions.

motFail: Gearbox faults do not typically cause a direct MOT failure unless they affect the operation of the vehicle in a way that makes it unsafe. However, associated warning lights or driveability issues may be noted.

name.fr: Capteur de regime en entree de transmission — circuit defaillant
name.en: Gearbox input speed sensor — circuit fault

description.fr: Le P0715 concerne le capteur qui mesure la vitesse de rotation de l'arbre d'entree de la boite automatique (turbine). Ce capteur permet au calculateur de boite de connaitre la vitesse a laquelle le convertisseur de couple tourne et donc de determiner le rapport engage et le patinage du convertisseur. Sans cette information, la boite ne sait plus ou elle en est et passe en mode degrade. Le mode degrade signifie generalement que la boite reste bloquee en 2e ou 3e vitesse pour permettre au conducteur de se rendre au garage. Le capteur est un element magnetique monte sur le carter de la boite de vitesses.
description.en: A P0715 fault code has been logged by your gearbox control module, indicating an issue with gearbox input speed sensor — circuit fault. This is a transmission-related fault that warrants prompt attention. Your automatic gearbox uses a network of sensors, solenoid valves, and hydraulic circuits to deliver smooth gear changes. When any of these components develop a fault, the gearbox ECU stores a code and may activate limp mode as a precaution. In limp mode, the gearbox typically locks into a single gear — usually second or third — to prevent further damage while still allowing you to drive to a garage. Symptoms you may have noticed include jerky gear changes, the gearbox slipping, delayed engagement when selecting drive or reverse, or the car revving high before changing gear. A transmission specialist will be able to diagnose the issue properly using dedicated equipment. The fix might be relatively simple, such as replacing a speed sensor or a solenoid valve, or it could indicate a more significant internal problem. Getting an accurate diagnosis early on can prevent a minor issue from turning into a major and expensive repair.

fr.question: La boite en mode degrade, c'est quoi exactement ?
fr.answer: Le calculateur bloque la boite sur un rapport unique (generalement 2e ou 3e) pour eviter d'endommager la mecanique. La conduite est possible mais limitee en vitesse et en acceleration.

fr.question: Ce code peut-il indiquer un probleme interne de boite ?
fr.answer: Oui, si le capteur est bon et que le probleme persiste, des composants internes comme le convertisseur de couple ou les embrayages peuvent etre en cause. Un diagnostic approfondi est necessaire.

fr.question: La reparation est-elle accessible ?
fr.answer: Le capteur lui-meme est souvent accessible de l'exterieur du carter de boite. Si c'est juste le capteur, la reparation est rapide et abordable. Un probleme interne est une autre histoire.

en.question: What does the P0715 fault code mean?
en.answer: The P0715 code means your car's computer has detected a problem with gearbox input speed sensor — circuit fault. This is a high-severity gearbox fault. Your mechanic can use an OBD2 diagnostic scanner to investigate further and identify the specific cause.

en.question: Can I drive with a P0715 code?
en.answer: You can drive short distances with this code, but it is advisable to get it checked soon. This is a high-severity fault that could worsen over time. Avoid long motorway journeys until a mechanic has had a look, and keep an eye on your temperature gauge and warning lights.

en.question: Will P0715 fail my MOT?
en.answer: Gearbox faults do not typically cause a direct MOT failure unless they affect the operation of the vehicle in a way that makes it unsafe. However, associated warning lights or driveability issues may be noted.

en.question: How much does P0715 repair cost in the UK?
en.answer: In the UK, repair costs for a P0715 fault typically range from around £25 to £2119, depending on the root cause and your vehicle's make and model. The most common fix is faulty input speed sensor, but a proper diagnostic is needed to confirm exactly what needs doing. Labour rates vary across the country, with London and the South East generally being more expensive than other regions.

motFail: Gearbox faults do not typically cause a direct MOT failure unless they affect the operation of the vehicle in a way that makes it unsafe. However, associated warning lights or driveability issues may be noted.

name.fr: Capteur de regime en sortie de transmission — circuit defaillant
name.en: Gearbox output speed sensor — circuit fault

description.fr: Le P0720 concerne le capteur de vitesse en sortie de la boite automatique, aussi appele capteur de vitesse de sortie (OSS). Ce capteur mesure la vitesse de l'arbre de sortie de la boite, ce qui correspond a la vitesse du vehicule. En combinaison avec le capteur d'entree, il permet au calculateur de determiner le rapport engage et le patinage eventuel. Un signal absent ou erratique provoque des changements de rapport inappropries, des a-coups et un passage en mode degrade. Le capteur est monte sur le carter de boite, generalement accessible sans demonter la boite elle-meme. La rouille, les vibrations et les fuites d'huile de boite sont les ennemis principaux de ce capteur.
description.en: The P0720 diagnostic code indicates a problem with gearbox output speed sensor — circuit fault in your gearbox system. Modern automatic and semi-automatic gearboxes are controlled by a dedicated computer that manages gear changes, torque converter operation, and various solenoid valves. When this computer detects an anomaly, it stores a fault code and may put the gearbox into limp mode — this is a safety feature that limits the car to one or two gears to protect the transmission from further damage. You might notice harsh or delayed gear changes, a reluctance to shift, or the car staying in a low gear regardless of speed. While it can be concerning, limp mode is actually your gearbox protecting itself. A specialist garage with gearbox diagnostic equipment can read the code and assess whether the issue is electrical — such as a faulty speed sensor or solenoid — or mechanical, like worn clutch packs or a failing torque converter. Gearbox repairs can be costly, so early diagnosis is essential. Sometimes a simple fluid change and solenoid replacement is all that is needed, saving you from a far more expensive rebuild.

fr.question: Ce capteur est-il le meme que le VSS ?
fr.answer: Sur certains vehicules oui, le capteur de sortie de boite sert aussi de capteur de vitesse vehicule. Sur d'autres, ce sont deux capteurs separes.

fr.question: Une fuite d'huile de boite peut-elle endommager le capteur ?
fr.answer: Oui, l'huile de boite qui s'infiltre dans le connecteur peut corroder les contacts et degrader le signal. Reparez la fuite et nettoyez le connecteur.

fr.question: Peut-on conduire en mode degrade longtemps ?
fr.answer: C'est prevu pour le depannage, pas pour la conduite quotidienne. En mode degrade, la boite chauffe plus car le rapport unique n'est pas optimal. Rendez-vous au garage rapidement.

en.question: What does the P0720 fault code mean?
en.answer: The P0720 code means your car's computer has detected a problem with gearbox output speed sensor — circuit fault. This is a high-severity gearbox fault. Your mechanic can use an OBD2 diagnostic scanner to investigate further and identify the specific cause.

en.question: Can I drive with a P0720 code?
en.answer: You can drive short distances with this code, but it is advisable to get it checked soon. This is a high-severity fault that could worsen over time. Avoid long motorway journeys until a mechanic has had a look, and keep an eye on your temperature gauge and warning lights.

en.question: Will P0720 fail my MOT?
en.answer: Gearbox faults do not typically cause a direct MOT failure unless they affect the operation of the vehicle in a way that makes it unsafe. However, associated warning lights or driveability issues may be noted.

en.question: How much does P0720 repair cost in the UK?
en.answer: In the UK, repair costs for a P0720 fault typically range from around £12 to £663, depending on the root cause and your vehicle's make and model. The most common fix is faulty output speed sensor, but a proper diagnostic is needed to confirm exactly what needs doing. Labour rates vary across the country, with London and the South East generally being more expensive than other regions.

motFail: Gearbox faults do not typically cause a direct MOT failure unless they affect the operation of the vehicle in a way that makes it unsafe. However, associated warning lights or driveability issues may be noted.

description.fr: Le P0730 indique que le calculateur de boite automatique detecte un rapport de vitesse incorrect. En comparant les vitesses d'entree et de sortie de la boite, il calcule un ratio qui ne correspond a aucun rapport programme. Cela peut signifier que les embrayages internes de la boite patinent, qu'un corps de vanne est defaillant ou que le niveau d'huile de boite est trop bas. C'est un code serieux qui indique un probleme mecanique ou hydraulique interne a la boite. Sur les boites a variation continue (CVT), ce code peut signaler un patinage de la courroie. Un diagnostic approfondi avec lecture des parametres en temps reel de la boite est indispensable.
description.en: A P0730 fault code has been logged by your gearbox control module, indicating an issue with incorrect gear ratio. This is a transmission-related fault that warrants prompt attention. Your automatic gearbox uses a network of sensors, solenoid valves, and hydraulic circuits to deliver smooth gear changes. When any of these components develop a fault, the gearbox ECU stores a code and may activate limp mode as a precaution. In limp mode, the gearbox typically locks into a single gear — usually second or third — to prevent further damage while still allowing you to drive to a garage. Symptoms you may have noticed include jerky gear changes, the gearbox slipping, delayed engagement when selecting drive or reverse, or the car revving high before changing gear. A transmission specialist will be able to diagnose the issue properly using dedicated equipment. The fix might be relatively simple, such as replacing a speed sensor or a solenoid valve, or it could indicate a more significant internal problem. Getting an accurate diagnosis early on can prevent a minor issue from turning into a major and expensive repair.

fr.question: Le P0730 est-il grave pour la boite ?
fr.answer: Oui, c'est un code qui indique un probleme mecanique ou hydraulique reel. Continuez a rouler risque d'aggraver les degats et d'augmenter significativement le cout de reparation.

fr.question: Verifier le niveau d'huile de boite peut-il aider ?
fr.answer: C'est la premiere chose a faire car c'est gratuit. Un niveau d'huile trop bas cause du patinage et des rapports incorrects. Certaines boites n'ont pas de jauge, il faut un outil de diagnostic pour lire la temperature et verifier le niveau.

fr.question: Combien coute la reparation d'une boite automatique ?
fr.answer: De 300 euros pour un corps de vanne a 3000 euros ou plus pour une revision complete avec remplacement des embrayages. C'est une des reparations les plus couteuses de l'automobile.

en.question: What does the P0730 fault code mean?
en.answer: The P0730 code means your car's computer has detected a problem with incorrect gear ratio. This is a high-severity gearbox fault. Your mechanic can use an OBD2 diagnostic scanner to investigate further and identify the specific cause.

en.question: Can I drive with a P0730 code?
en.answer: You can drive short distances with this code, but it is advisable to get it checked soon. This is a high-severity fault that could worsen over time. Avoid long motorway journeys until a mechanic has had a look, and keep an eye on your temperature gauge and warning lights.

en.question: Will P0730 fail my MOT?
en.answer: Gearbox faults do not typically cause a direct MOT failure unless they affect the operation of the vehicle in a way that makes it unsafe. However, associated warning lights or driveability issues may be noted.

en.question: How much does P0730 repair cost in the UK?
en.answer: In the UK, repair costs for a P0730 fault typically range from around £26 to £2515, depending on the root cause and your vehicle's make and model. The most common fix is worn internal clutches, but a proper diagnostic is needed to confirm exactly what needs doing. Labour rates vary across the country, with London and the South East generally being more expensive than other regions.

name.fr: Embrayage convertisseur de couple — circuit defaillant

description.fr: Le P0740 concerne l'embrayage de pontage du convertisseur de couple (TCC). Ce mecanisme verrouille le convertisseur de couple a haute vitesse pour eliminer le patinage et reduire la consommation. Si le circuit de commande est defaillant, l'embrayage ne se verrouille pas ou reste verrouille en permanence. Un embrayage qui ne se verrouille pas augmente la consommation de 5 a 10%. Un embrayage bloque en position verrouillee fait caler le moteur a l'arret. L'electrovanne TCC dans le corps de vanne est souvent en cause, mais le cablage et le calculateur de boite sont aussi a verifier. Ce probleme est frequent sur les vehicules avec beaucoup de kilometres en ville.
description.en: The P0740 diagnostic code indicates a problem with torque converter clutch — circuit fault in your gearbox system. Modern automatic and semi-automatic gearboxes are controlled by a dedicated computer that manages gear changes, torque converter operation, and various solenoid valves. When this computer detects an anomaly, it stores a fault code and may put the gearbox into limp mode — this is a safety feature that limits the car to one or two gears to protect the transmission from further damage. You might notice harsh or delayed gear changes, a reluctance to shift, or the car staying in a low gear regardless of speed. While it can be concerning, limp mode is actually your gearbox protecting itself. A specialist garage with gearbox diagnostic equipment can read the code and assess whether the issue is electrical — such as a faulty speed sensor or solenoid — or mechanical, like worn clutch packs or a failing torque converter. Gearbox repairs can be costly, so early diagnosis is essential. Sometimes a simple fluid change and solenoid replacement is all that is needed, saving you from a far more expensive rebuild.

fr.question: L'embrayage du convertisseur, c'est la meme chose que l'embrayage classique ?
fr.answer: Non, c'est specifique aux boites automatiques. Le convertisseur de couple transmet la puissance par un fluide. L'embrayage de pontage le verrouille mecaniquement pour plus d'efficacite a haute vitesse.

fr.question: Pourquoi le moteur cale-t-il si le TCC est bloque ?
fr.answer: Normalement, le convertisseur permet au moteur de tourner meme quand la voiture est a l'arret (grace au fluide). Si l'embrayage est bloque, le moteur est directement couple aux roues et cale quand la voiture s'arrete.

fr.question: L'electrovanne TCC est-elle accessible ?
fr.answer: Elle est dans le corps de vanne a l'interieur de la boite. L'acces necessite generalement de demonter le carter d'huile de la boite, ce qui est faisable mais demande de l'experience.

en.question: What does the P0740 fault code mean?

However, you may notice some changes in performance or fuel consumption. Book a diagnostic appointment when convenient — the sooner you get it sorted, the less likely it is to cause further issues.

Labour rates vary across the country, with London and the South East generally being more expensive than other regions.

motFail: Gearbox faults do not typically cause a direct MOT failure unless they affect the operation of the vehicle in a way that makes it unsafe. However, associated warning lights or driveability issues may be noted.

description.fr: Le P0750 concerne l'electrovanne de passage A dans le corps de vanne hydraulique de la boite automatique. Les electrovannes controlent les circuits hydrauliques qui engagent les differents rapports. Si l'electrovanne A est defaillante, certains rapports ne sont plus accessibles ou les changements de rapports sont brutaux. Le corps de vanne contient typiquement 4 a 8 electrovannes, chacune pilotant un circuit hydraulique specifique. L'electrovanne peut etre collee par des depots, avoir un bobinage grille ou un tiroir hydraulique bloque. Un changement d'huile de boite avec un filtre neuf peut parfois resoudre le probleme si c'est un encrassement. Si l'electrovanne est electriquement defaillante, il faut la remplacer.
description.en: A P0750 fault code has been logged by your gearbox control module, indicating an issue with shift solenoid a — malfunction. This is a transmission-related fault that warrants prompt attention. Your automatic gearbox uses a network of sensors, solenoid valves, and hydraulic circuits to deliver smooth gear changes. When any of these components develop a fault, the gearbox ECU stores a code and may activate limp mode as a precaution. In limp mode, the gearbox typically locks into a single gear — usually second or third — to prevent further damage while still allowing you to drive to a garage. Symptoms you may have noticed include jerky gear changes, the gearbox slipping, delayed engagement when selecting drive or reverse, or the car revving high before changing gear. A transmission specialist will be able to diagnose the issue properly using dedicated equipment. The fix might be relatively simple, such as replacing a speed sensor or a solenoid valve, or it could indicate a more significant internal problem. Getting an accurate diagnosis early on can prevent a minor issue from turning into a major and expensive repair.

fr.question: Peut-on nettoyer une electrovanne de boite ?
fr.answer: Parfois oui, avec un nettoyage du corps de vanne et un rincage a l'huile neuve. Si le bobinage est grille, seul le remplacement fonctionne.

fr.question: Faut-il remplacer tout le corps de vanne ?
fr.answer: Pas necessairement. L'electrovanne seule est souvent remplacable. Mais si le corps de vanne a subi un encrassement severe, le remplacement complet est plus fiable.

fr.question: L'huile de boite sale cause-t-elle ce probleme ?
fr.answer: Oui, une huile vieille et degradee depose des particules qui bloquent les electrovannes. C'est pourquoi le changement regulier de l'huile de boite est important, meme si certains constructeurs la qualifient de remplissage a vie.

name.fr: Capteur de vitesse de roue avant gauche — circuit defaillant
name.en: Front left wheel speed sensor — circuit fault

description.fr: Le C0035 signale un probleme sur le capteur ABS de la roue avant gauche. Ce capteur mesure la vitesse de rotation de la roue pour que le systeme ABS puisse detecter un blocage imminent lors du freinage. Sans le signal de cette roue, l'ABS est desactive car le calculateur ne peut plus comparer les vitesses entre les quatre roues. Le capteur est monte sur le moyeu ou l'etrier et lit une bague dentee fixee sur le disque ou le cardan. La corrosion, les debris de route et l'usure mecanique sont les causes principales. Le cablage du capteur chemine le long du bras de suspension et est expose aux projections et aux torsions lors des mouvements de roue.
description.en: The C0035 fault code relates to front left wheel speed sensor — circuit fault in your anti-lock braking system. The ABS is a crucial safety feature that prevents your wheels from locking up during heavy braking, allowing you to maintain steering control in emergency situations. When the ABS module detects a fault, it illuminates the ABS warning light on your dashboard and stores this code. Your normal brakes will still work perfectly fine, but the ABS function may be deactivated until the fault is repaired. This means that in an emergency stop, your wheels could lock up on slippery surfaces, which reduces your ability to steer. Common causes of ABS faults include dirty or damaged wheel speed sensors, corroded wiring, a faulty ABS ring on the wheel hub, or problems with the ABS hydraulic unit itself. A mechanic can diagnose the issue by reading the fault code and checking the sensor signals. Many ABS repairs are relatively straightforward and involve cleaning or replacing a sensor. It is worth noting that the MOT test does check the ABS warning light, so this needs fixing before your next test.

fr.question: Puis-je freiner normalement sans l'ABS ?
fr.answer: Oui, le systeme de freinage classique fonctionne toujours. Mais en cas de freinage d'urgence sur sol glissant, les roues risquent de bloquer car l'ABS ne pourra pas intervenir.

fr.question: Le capteur ABS et le capteur de vitesse de roue, c'est pareil ?
fr.answer: Oui, c'est le meme capteur. Il sert a la fois a l'ABS, a l'ESP, au compteur de vitesse et parfois au systeme de surveillance de pression des pneus.

fr.question: La bague ABS, c'est quoi ?
fr.answer: C'est un anneau dente en metal ou magnetique fixe sur le moyeu ou le cardan. Le capteur lit les dents pour compter les tours de la roue. Si elle est fissuree ou couverte de rouille, le signal est perturbe.

en.question: How much does C0035 repair cost in the UK?
en.answer: In the UK, repair costs for a C0035 fault typically range from around £25 to £130, depending on the root cause and your vehicle's make and model. The most common fix is front left abs sensor, but a proper diagnostic is needed to confirm exactly what needs doing. Labour rates vary across the country, with London and the South East generally being more expensive than other regions.

name.fr: Capteur de vitesse de roue avant droite — circuit defaillant
name.en: Front right wheel speed sensor — circuit fault

description.fr: Le C0040 est l'equivalent du C0035 pour la roue avant droite. Le capteur ABS de cette roue ne fonctionne plus correctement. Les memes causes s'appliquent : capteur defaillant, bague dentee endommagee ou cablage coupe. Le cote droit est parfois plus expose a l'eau et aux projections selon la configuration de la route et le sens de circulation. Sur les vehicules avec direction a droite, c'est le cote du conducteur qui est generalement le plus sollicite. Le voyant ABS et souvent le voyant ESP s'allument ensemble car l'ESP ne peut pas fonctionner sans l'information de toutes les roues. Le remplacement du capteur est une operation relativement simple.
description.en: Your car has logged a C0040 code, which indicates a problem with front right wheel speed sensor — circuit fault in the anti-lock braking system. Do not panic — your regular brakes are still working normally, but the ABS safety feature may be partially or fully deactivated. The ABS system uses sensors at each wheel to monitor rotation speed. If one wheel starts to lock during braking, the system rapidly pulses the brake on that wheel to maintain grip and steering control. When a sensor or its wiring develops a fault, the system cannot function correctly and switches itself off as a safety precaution. The ABS warning light on your dashboard confirms this. Typical causes include a damaged speed sensor, a cracked or dirty ABS ring, corroded connectors, or problems with the ABS module. Diagnosis usually involves scanning the codes and checking the sensor output with an oscilloscope. Repairs often come down to replacing a wheel speed sensor or cleaning a connector — both of which are fairly affordable. However, if the ABS hydraulic unit is at fault, costs can be higher. Either way, this is a safety-related system, so do get it checked at your earliest convenience, especially as it is part of the MOT test.

fr.question: L'ESP est-il aussi desactive ?
fr.answer: Oui, l'ESP a besoin de l'information de toutes les roues pour fonctionner. Si un seul capteur est defaillant, l'ESP est completement desactive.

fr.question: Le capteur est-il facile a remplacer ?
fr.answer: Generalement oui. Il est fixe par une vis ou un clip sur le moyeu. Le plus complique est parfois le demontage si la corrosion a soude le capteur en place. Un peu de degrippant aide.

fr.question: Peut-on rouler longtemps sans ABS ?
fr.answer: Ce n'est pas recommande. Pendant des decennies on a conduit sans ABS, mais les vehicules modernes sont concus autour de ce systeme. Les distances de freinage d'urgence sont significativement plus longues sans ABS.

description.fr: Le C0045 concerne le capteur ABS de la roue arriere gauche. Les capteurs arriere sont souvent plus exposes a la boue, l'eau et les projections de sel en hiver car ils sont plus proches du sol et moins proteges par la carrosserie. La bague ABS arriere est parfois integree au roulement de roue, ce qui signifie que si la bague est endommagee, il faut remplacer le roulement complet — une operation nettement plus couteuse. Certains vehicules ont des bagues magnetiques plutot que dentees, qui sont plus sensibles a la corrosion et aux debris metalliques. Le cablage arriere est plus long et traverse le dessous du vehicule, ce qui l'expose davantage aux dommages.

A mechanic can diagnose the issue by reading the fault code and checking the sensor signals. Many ABS repairs are relatively straightforward and involve cleaning or replacing a sensor. fr.answer: Sur beaucoup de vehicules recents oui. Verifiez aupres de votre garagiste. Si c'est le cas, le remplacement du roulement complet est necessaire, ce qui augmente le cout.

fr.question: Le sel de route endommage-t-il les capteurs ABS ?
fr.answer: Oui, le sel accelere la corrosion de tous les composants metalliques sous le vehicule, y compris les capteurs ABS, les connecteurs et la bague dentee.

fr.question: Comment savoir si c'est le capteur ou la bague ?
fr.answer: Un examen visuel de la bague permet de voir si elle est fissuree ou rouillée. Un test electrique du capteur au multimetre confirme s'il est en circuit ouvert ou en court-circuit.

description.fr: Le C0050 signale un defaut sur le capteur ABS de la roue arriere droite. Les problemes et solutions sont symetriques au C0045. Ce capteur est le quatrieme et dernier de l'ensemble ABS. Si un seul capteur est defaillant, tout le systeme ABS est desactive car il a besoin des quatre signaux pour fonctionner correctement. Sur certains vehicules utilitaires ou tout-terrain, les capteurs arriere sont encore plus exposes aux chocs et aux projections. Apres un remplacement de capteur ABS, il est parfois necessaire de faire un calibrage du systeme chez le garagiste pour que le calculateur reapprenne les signaux de reference de chaque roue.
description.en: Your car has logged a C0050 code, which indicates a problem with rear right wheel speed sensor — circuit fault in the anti-lock braking system. Do not panic — your regular brakes are still working normally, but the ABS safety feature may be partially or fully deactivated. The ABS system uses sensors at each wheel to monitor rotation speed. Typical causes include a damaged speed sensor, a cracked or dirty ABS ring, corroded connectors, or problems with the ABS module. Repairs often come down to replacing a wheel speed sensor or cleaning a connector — both of which are fairly affordable. However, if the ABS hydraulic unit is at fault, costs can be higher. Either way, this is a safety-related system, so do get it checked at your earliest convenience, especially as it is part of the MOT test.

fr.question: Faut-il calibrer l'ABS apres remplacement du capteur ?
fr.answer: Sur certains vehicules oui. Le calculateur doit reapprendre les parametres de reference du nouveau capteur. Un outil de diagnostic est necessaire pour cette operation.

fr.question: Le frein a main est-il affecte ?
fr.answer: Non, le frein a main (ou frein de stationnement) est un systeme mecanique independant de l'ABS. Il fonctionne normalement meme si l'ABS est desactive.

fr.question: Tous les capteurs ABS sont-ils identiques ?
fr.answer: Non, les capteurs avant et arriere sont souvent differents (longueur de cable, fixation, type de signal). Meme entre gauche et droite, le cablage peut avoir une longueur differente.

description.fr: Le C0060 pointe un probleme sur une electrovanne a l'interieur du bloc hydraulique ABS. Le bloc ABS contient des electrovannes qui modulent la pression de freinage independamment sur chaque roue lors d'un freinage d'urgence. Si une electrovanne est bloquee ou ne repond plus, l'ABS ne peut plus reduire la pression sur la roue qui bloque. Le bloc hydraulique est un composant couteux car il integre les electrovannes, la pompe et l'unite electronique. Cependant, des specialistes en reparation de blocs ABS existent et peuvent souvent reparer l'unite pour 30 a 50% du prix d'un remplacement neuf. Le defaut peut etre intermittent au debut, provoquant des interventions ABS inattendues ou un voyant qui s'allume puis s'eteint.
description.en: The C0060 fault code relates to abs hydraulic unit — solenoid fault in your anti-lock braking system. The ABS is a crucial safety feature that prevents your wheels from locking up during heavy braking, allowing you to maintain steering control in emergency situations. When the ABS module detects a fault, it illuminates the ABS warning light on your dashboard and stores this code. Your normal brakes will still work perfectly fine, but the ABS function may be deactivated until the fault is repaired. This means that in an emergency stop, your wheels could lock up on slippery surfaces, which reduces your ability to steer. Common causes of ABS faults include dirty or damaged wheel speed sensors, corroded wiring, a faulty ABS ring on the wheel hub, or problems with the ABS hydraulic unit itself. A mechanic can diagnose the issue by reading the fault code and checking the sensor signals. Many ABS repairs are relatively straightforward and involve cleaning or replacing a sensor. It is worth noting that the MOT test does check the ABS warning light, so this needs fixing before your next test.

fr.question: Le bloc ABS est-il reparable ?
fr.answer: Oui, des specialistes comme BBA Reman ou ACtronics peuvent reparer les blocs ABS. C'est moins cher qu'un remplacement neuf et tout aussi fiable.

fr.question: L'ABS peut-il freiner une roue tout seul ?
fr.answer: Non, l'ABS ne freine pas — il module la pression que vous appliquez. Il ne peut pas ajouter de pression, seulement la reduire momentanement pour eviter le blocage.

fr.question: Pourquoi le bloc ABS est-il si cher ?
fr.answer: C'est un composant de precision qui integre des electrovannes, une pompe electrique et un module electronique dans un bloc d'aluminium usine. La fabrication est complexe et les tolerances tres strictes.

name.fr: Pompe de retour ABS — circuit defaillant

description.fr: Le C0065 signale un probleme sur le circuit electrique de la pompe du bloc ABS. Cette pompe sert a remonter la pression dans le circuit de freinage apres que les electrovannes l'ont reduite pendant une intervention ABS. Si la pompe ne fonctionne pas, l'ABS ne peut pas faire de cycles pression-relachement-pression qui permettent de freiner sans bloquer les roues. Le moteur electrique de la pompe peut etre grille, ou le relais qui l'alimente peut etre defaillant. Un bruit de ronronnement venant du bloc ABS quand la pompe tourne en permanence peut aussi indiquer un probleme. Ce code desactive completement l'ABS.
description.en: Your car has logged a C0065 code, which indicates a problem with abs return pump — circuit fault in the anti-lock braking system. Do not panic — your regular brakes are still working normally, but the ABS safety feature may be partially or fully deactivated. The ABS system uses sensors at each wheel to monitor rotation speed. If one wheel starts to lock during braking, the system rapidly pulses the brake on that wheel to maintain grip and steering control. When a sensor or its wiring develops a fault, the system cannot function correctly and switches itself off as a safety precaution. The ABS warning light on your dashboard confirms this. Typical causes include a damaged speed sensor, a cracked or dirty ABS ring, corroded connectors, or problems with the ABS module. Diagnosis usually involves scanning the codes and checking the sensor output with an oscilloscope. Repairs often come down to replacing a wheel speed sensor or cleaning a connector — both of which are fairly affordable. However, if the ABS hydraulic unit is at fault, costs can be higher. Either way, this is a safety-related system, so do get it checked at your earliest convenience, especially as it is part of the MOT test.

name.en: Wiring of the engine of pompe

fr.question: La pompe ABS tourne-t-elle en permanence ?
fr.answer: Non, normalement elle ne tourne que pendant une intervention ABS (freinage d'urgence). Si vous l'entendez tourner souvent ou en permanence, c'est un signe de defaillance.

fr.question: Le relais est-il le meme que celui de la pompe a carburant ?
fr.answer: Non, c'est un relais dedie au systeme ABS. Il est generalement dans la boite a fusibles sous le capot. Consultez le schema pour l'identifier.

fr.question: Peut-on rouler sans la pompe ABS ?
fr.answer: Oui, le freinage classique fonctionne normalement. Seule la fonction antiblocage est perdue. Soyez plus prudent sur sol mouille ou glissant.

en.question: What does the C0065 fault code mean?
en.answer: The C0065 code means your car's computer has detected a problem with abs return pump — circuit fault. This is a high-severity ABS braking fault. Your mechanic can use an OBD2 diagnostic scanner to investigate further and identify the specific cause.

en.question: Can I drive with a C0065 code?
en.answer: You can drive short distances with this code, but it is advisable to get it checked soon. This is a high-severity fault that could worsen over time. Avoid long motorway journeys until a mechanic has had a look, and keep an eye on your temperature gauge and warning lights.

en.question: Will C0065 fail my MOT?
en.answer: Yes, the ABS warning light is checked during the MOT test. If the ABS light is illuminated, this will result in a fail. The ABS system must be functioning correctly to pass.

en.question: How much does C0065 repair cost in the UK?
en.answer: In the UK, repair costs for a C0065 fault typically range from around £17 to £1021, depending on the root cause and your vehicle's make and model. The most common fix is faulty abs pump, but a proper diagnostic is needed to confirm exactly what needs doing. Labour rates vary across the country, with London and the South East generally being more expensive than other regions.

motFail: Yes, the ABS warning light is checked during the MOT test. If the ABS light is illuminated, this will result in a fail. The ABS system must be functioning correctly to pass.

name.fr: Module airbag conducteur — resistance hors plage
name.en: Driver airbag module — resistance out of range

description.fr: Le B0001 indique que le calculateur airbag mesure une resistance anormale sur le circuit de l'airbag conducteur, celui qui est dans le volant. La resistance du circuit doit etre dans une plage tres precise pour garantir que l'airbag se deploiera correctement en cas d'accident. Une resistance trop haute peut empecher le deploiement, une resistance trop basse peut provoquer un deploiement involontaire. Le ressort spirale (ou contacteur tournant) dans la colonne de direction est souvent en cause car il s'use avec les rotations du volant. Le connecteur du volant, qui subit des vibrations constantes, peut aussi se desserrer. C'est un code a ne pas ignorer car il affecte directement votre securite passive.
description.en: The B0001 fault code is a serious one — it relates to driver airbag module — resistance out of range in your supplemental restraint system, commonly known as the airbag system. When the airbag warning light is illuminated on your dashboard, it means the airbag control module has detected a fault that could prevent one or more airbags from deploying correctly in a collision. This is obviously a significant safety concern, and while the car is perfectly safe to drive in terms of engine and braking performance, you should be aware that your passive safety protection may be compromised. Common causes include a faulty clock spring behind the steering wheel, a damaged wiring connector under a seat, a worn seat occupancy sensor, or an issue with the airbag module itself. Airbag repairs should always be carried out by a qualified technician because the system contains explosive charges. Never attempt to work on airbag components yourself. A diagnostic scan will identify exactly which part of the system is at fault, and your mechanic can then advise on the repair. The MOT test does check the airbag warning light, so this will need to be resolved before your next test.

en: Horn or steering wheel controls malfunctioning

fr.question: Mon airbag va-t-il se deployer avec ce code ?
fr.answer: Impossible a garantir. Le voyant airbag allume signifie que le systeme a detecte un defaut et que le deploiement normal n'est pas garanti. Faites reparer rapidement.

fr.question: Le ressort spirale, c'est quoi ?
fr.answer: C'est un ruban conducteur enroule qui maintient la connexion electrique entre le volant (qui tourne) et la colonne de direction (qui ne tourne pas). Il transmet le courant aux airbags, au klaxon et aux commandes au volant.

fr.question: Est-ce dangereux de rouler avec le voyant airbag ?
fr.answer: Le freinage et la conduite ne sont pas affectes, mais en cas d'accident, l'airbag pourrait ne pas se deployer. C'est un risque pour votre vie et celle de vos passagers.

en.question: What does the B0001 fault code mean?
en.answer: The B0001 code means your car's computer has detected a problem with driver airbag module — resistance out of range. This is a critical-severity airbag safety fault. Your mechanic can use an OBD2 diagnostic scanner to investigate further and identify the specific cause.

en.question: Can I drive with a B0001 code?
en.answer: While the car may still run, this is a critical fault and you should drive with extreme caution. Limit your driving to essential journeys only and get to a garage as soon as possible. Continuing to drive could cause further damage or put your safety at risk.

en.question: Will B0001 fail my MOT?
en.answer: Yes, the airbag warning light is checked during the MOT test. An illuminated airbag light is an automatic MOT failure. The supplemental restraint system must show no warning lights to pass.

en.question: How much does B0001 repair cost in the UK?
en.answer: In the UK, repair costs for a B0001 fault typically range from around £26 to £498, depending on the root cause and your vehicle's make and model. The most common fix is clock spring (rotary coupler), but a proper diagnostic is needed to confirm exactly what needs doing. Labour rates vary across the country, with London and the South East generally being more expensive than other regions.

motFail: Yes, the airbag warning light is checked during the MOT test. An illuminated airbag light is an automatic MOT failure. The supplemental restraint system must show no warning lights to pass.

name.fr: Module airbag passager — resistance hors plage
name.en: Passenger airbag module — resistance out of range

description.fr: Le B0002 concerne l'airbag du cote passager, generalement situe dans le tableau de bord. Comme pour le B0001, la resistance du circuit est hors de la plage acceptable. Les causes sont similaires mais le connecteur est different car l'airbag passager n'est pas sur une partie tournante. Le connecteur sous le tableau de bord peut se desserrer avec les vibrations, ou le faisceau peut etre endommage par un objet place sous le siege. Sur certains vehicules avec desactivation d'airbag passager, un contact defaillant de desactivation peut aussi provoquer ce code. Le voyant airbag restera allume tant que le probleme n'est pas resolu.
description.en: Your vehicle has stored a B0002 code, indicating a fault with passenger airbag module — resistance out of range in the airbag system. The airbag warning light on your dashboard will be illuminated, signalling that the system may not function as intended in the event of an accident. While this does not affect the drivability of your car, it is a critical safety concern that should be addressed as soon as possible. The airbag system is made up of multiple components — crash sensors, the airbag control module, wiring harnesses, and the airbag units themselves. A fault in any of these can trigger a warning code. Common culprits include damaged wiring under the front seats, a worn clock spring in the steering column, a faulty seat occupancy sensor, or a connector that has become corroded over time. Due to the explosive nature of airbag components, repairs must be carried out by a trained professional. Your mechanic will use a specialist scanner to identify the specific fault and recommend the appropriate repair. Remember that an illuminated airbag light will result in an MOT failure, so this needs attention before your next test date.

fr.question: Mon passager est-il en danger ?
fr.answer: Si le voyant airbag est allume, l'airbag passager pourrait ne pas se deployer en cas de choc. C'est un risque reel pour le passager avant.

fr.question: Le bouton de desactivation airbag passager est-il en cause ?
fr.answer: C'est possible. Sur les vehicules avec ce bouton (souvent cote boite a gants), un contact defaillant peut fausser la lecture de resistance et allumer le voyant.

fr.question: Faut-il passer au concessionnaire pour l'airbag ?
fr.answer: Pas obligatoirement, mais les systemes airbag sont sensibles et necessitent un diagnostic precis. Un garagiste equipe d'un outil capable de lire les codes SRS peut intervenir.

en.question: What does the B0002 fault code mean?
en.answer: The B0002 code means your car's computer has detected a problem with passenger airbag module — resistance out of range. This is a critical-severity airbag safety fault. Your mechanic can use an OBD2 diagnostic scanner to investigate further and identify the specific cause.

en.answer: While the car may still run, this is a critical fault and you should drive with extreme caution. Limit your driving to essential journeys only and get to a garage as soon as possible. Continuing to drive could cause further damage or put your safety at risk.

The most common fix is faulty passenger airbag module, but a proper diagnostic is needed to confirm exactly what needs doing. Labour rates vary across the country, with London and the South East generally being more expensive than other regions.

motFail: Yes, the airbag warning light is checked during the MOT test. An illuminated airbag light is an automatic MOT failure. The supplemental restraint system must show no warning lights to pass.

name.fr: Pretensionneur de ceinture conducteur — circuit defaillant

description.fr: Le B0003 signale un defaut sur le circuit du pretensionneur de ceinture du conducteur. Le pretensionneur est un mecanisme pyrotechnique integre a la ceinture de securite qui resserre brutalement la ceinture au moment d'un choc pour maintenir le conducteur en place avant que l'airbag ne se deploie. Si le circuit est defaillant, le pretensionneur pourrait ne pas se declencher lors d'un accident. Le connecteur sous le siege est souvent en cause car il subit les mouvements du siege et les vibrations. Un objet coince sous le siege peut aussi endommager le cablage. Apres un accident meme mineur, les pretensionneurs qui se sont declenches doivent etre remplaces.
description.en: The B0003 fault code is a serious one — it relates to driver seatbelt pretensioner — circuit fault in your supplemental restraint system, commonly known as the airbag system. When the airbag warning light is illuminated on your dashboard, it means the airbag control module has detected a fault that could prevent one or more airbags from deploying correctly in a collision. This is obviously a significant safety concern, and while the car is perfectly safe to drive in terms of engine and braking performance, you should be aware that your passive safety protection may be compromised. Common causes include a faulty clock spring behind the steering wheel, a damaged wiring connector under a seat, a worn seat occupancy sensor, or an issue with the airbag module itself. Airbag repairs should always be carried out by a qualified technician because the system contains explosive charges. Never attempt to work on airbag components yourself. A diagnostic scan will identify exactly which part of the system is at fault, and your mechanic can then advise on the repair. The MOT test does check the airbag warning light, so this will need to be resolved before your next test.

fr.question: C'est quoi un pretensionneur de ceinture ?
fr.answer: C'est un dispositif explosif miniature dans l'enrouleur de ceinture. En cas de choc, il se declenche en quelques millisecondes pour tendre la ceinture et plaquer le conducteur au siege avant le deploiement de l'airbag.

fr.question: Apres un accident, faut-il remplacer le pretensionneur ?
fr.answer: Oui, s'il s'est declenche. C'est un dispositif a usage unique, comme un airbag. Meme si l'accident etait mineur, si le pretensionneur a tire, il doit etre remplace.

fr.question: Un objet sous le siege peut-il causer ce code ?
fr.answer: Oui, une bouteille, un parapluie ou tout objet glisse sous le siege peut pincer ou endommager le connecteur du pretensionneur. Verifiez regulierement sous vos sieges.

en.question: What does the B0003 fault code mean?
en.answer: The B0003 code means your car's computer has detected a problem with driver seatbelt pretensioner — circuit fault. This is a critical-severity airbag safety fault. Your mechanic can use an OBD2 diagnostic scanner to investigate further and identify the specific cause.

description.fr: Le B0010 concerne l'airbag lateral du cote conducteur, generalement loge dans le dossier du siege ou dans le montant de portiere. Cet airbag protege le torse et la tete en cas de choc lateral. Le circuit electrique de cet airbag est defaillant — resistance hors plage, circuit ouvert ou court-circuit. Le connecteur sous le siege, qui doit permettre le mouvement du siege tout en maintenant le contact, est une cause frequente de problemes. Les housses de siege montees par-dessus les airbags lateraux peuvent aussi interferer avec le systeme en appuyant sur le connecteur ou en bloquant le deploiement. Ce code desactive le deploiement de l'airbag lateral conducteur.
description.en: Your vehicle has stored a B0010 code, indicating a fault with driver side airbag — circuit fault in the airbag system. The airbag warning light on your dashboard will be illuminated, signalling that the system may not function as intended in the event of an accident. While this does not affect the drivability of your car, it is a critical safety concern that should be addressed as soon as possible. The airbag system is made up of multiple components — crash sensors, the airbag control module, wiring harnesses, and the airbag units themselves. A fault in any of these can trigger a warning code. Common culprits include damaged wiring under the front seats, a worn clock spring in the steering column, a faulty seat occupancy sensor, or a connector that has become corroded over time. Due to the explosive nature of airbag components, repairs must be carried out by a trained professional. Your mechanic will use a specialist scanner to identify the specific fault and recommend the appropriate repair. Remember that an illuminated airbag light will result in an MOT failure, so this needs attention before your next test date.

fr.question: Les housses de siege affectent-elles les airbags ?
fr.answer: Oui, des housses non adaptees peuvent bloquer le deploiement de l'airbag lateral ou interferer avec le connecteur. Utilisez uniquement des housses certifiees compatibles airbag.

fr.question: L'airbag lateral protege-t-il la tete ?
fr.answer: Ca depend des modeles. Certains airbags lateraux protegent uniquement le torse, d'autres sont combines avec un airbag rideau pour la tete. Les airbags rideaux sont generalement dans le pavillon.

fr.question: Un choc lateral est-il plus dangereux qu'un choc frontal ?
fr.answer: Oui, car l'espace de deformation est beaucoup plus reduit. C'est pourquoi les airbags lateraux sont essentiels et pourquoi il ne faut pas ignorer ce code.

en.question: What does the B0010 fault code mean?

en.question: Can I drive with a B0010 code?

en.question: How much does B0010 repair cost in the UK?
en.answer: In the UK, repair costs for a B0010 fault typically range from around £26 to £416, depending on the root cause and your vehicle's make and model. The most common fix is connector airbag lateral, but a proper diagnostic is needed to confirm exactly what needs doing. Labour rates vary across the country, with London and the South East generally being more expensive than other regions.

motFail: Yes, the airbag warning light is checked during the MOT test. An illuminated airbag light is an automatic MOT failure. The supplemental restraint system must show no warning lights to pass.

name.fr: Capteur de temperature interieure — circuit defaillant

description.fr: Le B1420 indique que le capteur de temperature a l'interieur de l'habitacle ne fonctionne plus. Ce capteur, souvent situe dans le tableau de bord ou pres du retroviseur interieur, mesure la temperature de l'habitacle pour que la climatisation automatique puisse maintenir la temperature souhaitee. Sans cette information, la climatisation ne peut plus regler automatiquement la puissance de chauffage ou de refroidissement. Elle peut souffler trop chaud ou trop froid de maniere aleatoire. Le capteur lui-meme est un petit composant peu couteux, mais il est parfois difficile d'acces. Un petit ventilateur aspire l'air de l'habitacle pour le faire passer sur le capteur — si ce ventilateur est bloque, le capteur ne mesure pas la bonne temperature.
description.en: The B1420 fault code indicates a problem with interior temperature sensor — circuit fault in your air conditioning system. While A/C faults are not as urgent as engine or brake issues, they can make driving uncomfortable, especially during warmer months. Your car's climate control system uses a network of sensors to monitor interior and exterior temperatures, refrigerant pressure, and compressor operation. When one of these sensors or components develops a fault, the system may not regulate temperature properly, the A/C might blow only warm air, or it might cut out intermittently. Common causes include a faulty temperature sensor, a refrigerant leak, a worn compressor clutch, or a clogged condenser. A garage equipped with A/C diagnostic tools can identify the specific issue quickly. Many A/C repairs are quite affordable — a sensor replacement or a re-gas might be all that is needed. More significant repairs like compressor replacement cost more, but they are still manageable. While this fault will not cause your car to fail its MOT directly, having a properly functioning climate control system makes for much more pleasant driving, particularly during the British summer and winter months.

fr.question: Ma clim souffle tantot chaud tantot froid, est-ce ca ?
fr.answer: C'est probable. Sans la mesure de temperature interieure, la climatisation ne sait pas si elle doit chauffer ou refroidir et fait des corrections erratiques.

fr.question: Le ventilateur du capteur, c'est quoi ?
fr.answer: C'est un minuscule ventilateur integre au capteur qui aspire l'air de l'habitacle pour une mesure representative. S'il est bloque par la poussiere, le capteur mesure sa propre temperature au lieu de celle de l'air.

fr.question: Puis-je utiliser la clim en mode manuel ?
fr.answer: Oui, le mode manuel fonctionne independamment du capteur. Vous regler la vitesse de ventilation et la temperature vous-meme. Seul le mode automatique est affecte.

en.question: What does the B1420 fault code mean?
en.answer: The B1420 code means your car's computer has detected a problem with interior temperature sensor — circuit fault. This is a low-severity air conditioning fault. Your mechanic can use an OBD2 diagnostic scanner to investigate further and identify the specific cause.

en.question: Can I drive with a B1420 code?
en.answer: Yes, you can generally continue driving with this code. It is a low-severity fault that should not cause immediate danger. However, you may notice some changes in performance or fuel consumption. Book a diagnostic appointment when convenient — the sooner you get it sorted, the less likely it is to cause further issues.

en.question: Will B1420 fail my MOT?
en.answer: No, air conditioning faults do not cause an MOT failure. The A/C system is not part of the MOT test. However, if the fault affects the demister function, it could indirectly impact windscreen visibility checks.

en.question: How much does B1420 repair cost in the UK?
en.answer: In the UK, repair costs for a B1420 fault typically range from around £13 to £83, depending on the root cause and your vehicle's make and model. The most common fix is cabin temperature sensor, but a proper diagnostic is needed to confirm exactly what needs doing. Labour rates vary across the country, with London and the South East generally being more expensive than other regions.

motFail: No, air conditioning faults do not cause an MOT failure. The A/C system is not part of the MOT test. However, if the fault affects the demister function, it could indirectly impact windscreen visibility checks.

name.fr: Capteur de temperature exterieure — circuit defaillant

description.fr: Le B1440 concerne le capteur de temperature exterieure, situe generalement derriere le pare-chocs avant ou pres du retroviseur. Ce capteur indique la temperature de l'air ambiant, information utilisee par la climatisation pour anticiper les besoins de refroidissement ou de chauffage. Il est aussi utilise pour l'affichage de la temperature exterieure au tableau de bord et pour les alertes de verglas. Un capteur defaillant affiche souvent une temperature absurde comme -40 degres ou +80 degres. Le capteur est expose aux projections d'eau et de sel, ce qui accelere sa corrosion. L'impact sur la conduite est nul mais le confort climatique est degrade.
description.en: Your car has logged a B1440 code related to exterior temperature sensor — circuit fault in the climate control system. The air conditioning in modern vehicles is managed by a dedicated module that reads temperature and pressure sensors to maintain your chosen cabin temperature. When one of these sensors sends an abnormal signal, the system may not work correctly — you might find the A/C blowing warm air, the fan behaving oddly, or the system switching itself off. This kind of fault is usually down to a sensor failure, a wiring issue, or a problem with the refrigerant charge. A qualified technician can diagnose the issue using a combination of OBD2 scanning and manual checks on the A/C system pressure and component function. Repairs are typically straightforward and can range from a simple sensor swap to an A/C system re-gas or, in more serious cases, compressor replacement. While an A/C fault will not make your car unsafe to drive or cause an MOT failure, it can be a real nuisance, especially in extreme weather. Getting it fixed will restore your comfort and help keep the windscreen clear during winter, which is important for safe driving.

fr: Temperature exterieure aberrante au tableau de bord

fr.question: Mon afficheur indique -40 degres en plein ete, est-ce le capteur ?
fr.answer: Oui, c'est typique d'un capteur ou d'un cablage en circuit ouvert. Le calculateur affiche la valeur par defaut la plus basse.

fr.question: Le capteur est-il derriere le pare-chocs ?
fr.answer: Generalement oui, derriere la grille ou le pare-chocs avant. Il doit etre a l'abri du soleil direct et du rayonnement moteur pour mesurer l'air ambiant reel.

fr.question: L'alerte verglas depend-elle de ce capteur ?
fr.answer: Oui, l'alerte verglas (generalement a 3 degres) utilise ce capteur. Si le capteur est HS, vous ne recevrez pas d'alerte et ne verrez pas la temperature exterieure reelle.
